Title: [Authz] Update theme cache when resource type updates
[Silver Fern Farms] Incorrect Placement of "inventoryClassification.name" in the Datapoint Key of pfLivestockFinancialTransactionResource
[Silver Fern Farms] Date format differences in Standardized files (carcass obsv. and financial transactions)
[Silver Fern Farms] Livestock Financial Transactions DPK change
Description:
I want to include the Animal Category Code in the data point key for SFF and ANZCO Livestock Financial Transactions so that we can distinguish transactions in a killsheet for animals which have the same sex but different category.

For example, bulls and bobbys would currently have the same DPK because they map to the same species and sex.

The solution is to include [inventoryClassification.name] in the DPK for pfLivestockFinancialTransactionResource.
Note that "name": ["Killsheet #"-"Animal Category Code"]

The DPK is:
[transactionKind]_ [eventDateTime]_ [resourceReference.identifier.scheme]_ [resourceReference.identifier.id]_ [classificationId.scheme]_ [classificationId.id]_   [inventoryClassification.name]_ [inventoryClassification.species]_ [inventoryClassification.sex]_ [inventoryClassification.birthPeriod]_ [inventoryClassification.primaryBreed.scheme]_ [inventoryClassification.primaryBreed.id]_ [inventoryClassification.lactationStatus]
Acceptance Criteria: I can store livestock financial transactions from a killsheet for different livestock category codes but which have the same sex.

  2. Mark this ticket as blocked

Title: [Trev] Update Trev to fetch all events and filter in code
Description:
Background

Please note, if #34284 works, this ticket is not needed anymore.

Since #32643 we fetch Class Change In (AgedIn) and Class Change Out (AgedOut) from Trev for a given farm. However, those events are not required to exist on a given farm. Calling it for a farm that does not have the event in question will cause a 500 with a html response. (It seems like the API we are calling from Trev is their BFF). This HTML page does not give any indication on what has happened. To make Trev working for Craigmore's deadline (31st of October 2024) we just capture 500s and log them as warning (to avoid prod alerts all the time). The currently implemented way of capturing the 500 is also a precaution for other customers. Trev will respond in the same manner if the farm in question does not have births events available (which Craigmore's and Ngai Tahu's farms happened to have).

Implication

If there is an actual 500 that we would like to get notified on, we won't. The customer will find missing data and we will find the appropriate log line and say "Oh we logged it, but didn't alert ourselves. How stupid of us.". 

Proposed Solution

Ideally, we would change the ingestion to fetch all the livestock-events of the farm that was triggered via the data connection and filter them in code for the events. But this is a medium sized change and will need some good testing.

Currently, we use 
https://go.mytrev.com/api/v1/livestock-events?filter[event_type]={event_code}&filter[farm_id]={farm_id}

The proposed solution is to call 
https://go.mytrev.com/api/v1/livestock-events?filter[farm_id]={farm_id}

at the beginning of the ingestion and filter the event types in code in the returned livestock events.


Acceptance Criteria:
  • Ingestion does not throw exceptions if event type does not exist
  • Ingestion does throw exceptions for other 500 responses
  • The written events have not changed in comparison to before this change

FYI @Chris Lloyd @Miranda Hennebry @Matt McKinley @Wade Parker @Daniel Buchanan

Title: [ASB] ANZCO
[P3 Incident] Getting Data Connections for Source times out in Production
Description:
Ravensdown are seeing timeouts when hitting: https://api.purefarming.com/ecosystem/data-source/com.purefarming.data-source.ravensdown/connections.

This is because the overall execution of the handler is taking between 90 and 110 seconds, so between one and a half and two minutes.
Logs: https://350457777237-jppjoskd.ap-southeast-2.console.aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/home?region=ap-southeast-2#logsV2:log-groups/log-group/$252Flogs$252Fecs$252Fmoa-platform-output-ecosystem-prod-ap-southeast-2-task-definition/log-events/ecs$252Fecosystem$252Ff08ef96fdbc948208b5c8f75ccf1ffcc

This looks like a volume of data issue insofar as the the response from the data source service (underlying service responsible for data connections) is taking ~60s, the rest of the time is deserialising and re-organising of the models.

EDIT:
Chris and myself had a quick look. In the data source service there is a getConnectionsByDataSource endpoint. This will in the end call a MapConnections method, that really adds the dataSource information to each of those connections. Two questions that we may want to ask for a potential solution:
  • Since all those connections are for the same dataSource we could think about a way to reuse the once fetched connection
  • Or do we need the data source information here at all? Can we just omit it because the caller knows already about the data source (since this is part of the request parameters.)

Title: [Overseer] Write ingestion - Fetch
Description:
As a Farmer I want to be able to provide my Overseer data to a data consumer so that they can receive GHG results and enterprise statistics.

GET Find Publications by Farms    to get the "publications[].id"
GET {{url}}/api/v2/search/publicationsByFarm
We want the publication where "publications[].reference" = the holding id (this step is pending discussion in #35458)
(The farmer will be instructed to paste the holding id into Publishee's reference.)
Check for publications[].publishedOn field and compare with dataConnection's connectionParameters.lastPublishedOn.
  • Initially this field won't be present which means continue otherwise
  • only continue if publications[].publishedOn > connectionParameters.lastPublishedOn

GET Summary Input Data for a Publication to get the input data for the Publication
GET {{url}}/api/v2/publication-summary/:publicationId

Get Overseer Model Results for a Publication to get the published results
GET {{url}}/api/v2/model-results/overseer/:publicationId

Add the current publications[].publishedOn content to connectionParameters.lastPublishedOn for reference on the next run.

Create the following json object:
{
    "inputs": response of GET {{url}}/api/v2/publication-summary/:publicationId,
    "modelResults": response of GET GET {{url}}/api/v2/model-results/overseer/:publicationId
}

Save json object in a file in raw bucket with Key: overseer/date-of-ingestions/e_holdingId_u_universalId.json
Send fetch event to standardisation.
Acceptance Criteria:
• The files written to the raw bucket and the fetch event is published
  • When we fetch overseer data the first time for a farmer the connectionParamters get updated to hold the lastPublishedOn date
  • If a existing connection runs again and the publishedOn field has not changed the ingestion does not continue.

Title: [SilverFernFarms][ANZCO] Only output carcass weight in "transactionQuantity"
Description:
Change the ingestion to "pfLivestockFinancialTransactionResource" for both Silver Fern Farms and ANZCO so that the determination of "transactionQuantity" always uses carcass weight (that is, it is always the sum of "Total Weight" for the inventory classification).

Correspondingly "weightType" should only be set to "carcass".
This will alter the value in "weightPerHead" too because it is calculated from "transactionQuantity".
When calcuating the Weight Per Head only use the animals that have a weight value provided.

Currently the ingestion uses Live Weight if it is available in preference to using "Total Weight".

The mapping file and mapping notes have been updated accordingly.
Acceptance Criteria:
• The "transactionQuantity" calculation should only be based on "Total Weight".
  • The weightPerHead should be based on animals that have contributed to the total weight
  • "weightType" should always be "carcass"
  • This should apply for both Silver Fern Farms and ANZCO.

Title: [Overseer] Overseer publishing to be done by end consumer, not to Map of Ag
Description:
• Update the Entity Organisation with a MetaDataSourcesInformation which takes a list of key value pairs
    • This new value needs to be part of CRUD endpoints
  • Update MetaDataSourcesInformation of ASB Organisaton to be "Overseer":"ASB Bank" (in Prod only)
    • For non prod it should be set to Map of Ag for ASB
  • Update Provider and Ingestion Secret for Overseer to be "ASB Bank": 'stringified version of current secret' (in Prod only)
    • For non prod it should be set to Map of Ag for ASB
    • We may want to have a second secret (and different Organisation with another Overseer name) that contains the same credentials, for testing. This is because we can't get a second credentials pair from Overseer at the moment.
  • Update Provider to fetch Org Details in Overseer step and look up MetaDataSourcesInformation for Key Overseer
    • Provide this name in the UI in the instructions
    • Put this name in the data connection parameters as overseer_client
    • For the check if the farmer has published the data properly use the overseer_client to get the appropriate part of the secret and use the credentials to do the check
    • If the Consumer Organisation does not have an Overseer key in MetaDataSourcesInformation we will show a page saying "You cannot connect Overseer data for this organisation." (to be further discussed in #35811)
  • Make the API calls based on the overseer_client (found in connection parameters). Use this client to look up the credentials in the existing secret.
  • Update ASB Organisation and Secrets after pushing to the next environment.

Background:
In the UI to date, the publishee for an Overseer analysis has been set as "Map of Ag for ASB"

This doesn't work for any party
  • It doesn't allow us to scale the connector or approach to other Consumers.
  • It doesn't have a clear path for Overseer or Map of Ag to understand where data will be used.
  • Overseer controls which end consumer the data is published to. As these are individually published analyses, rather than farm transactional data, this is fair.
Potential Approach (proposed by Overseer)
  1. Use the Data Consumer org name of the Data Request to display the party for connection in the UI.
    For instance "ASB Bank". This will require coordination with Overseer to ensure that organisation is set up with the same name in Overseer as a potential publishing recipient. This is similar to what we have done with Rabobank for Fonterra Insights.
  2. Map of Ag to be issued separate Overseer API credentials for each data consumer organisation. We would need to consider how to do this. Overseer don't have a way to associate one API client with multiple publishing recipients. This would need to be used in both the UI when testing connection, plus in fetch.
#35458 
#35454

@Don Wilson @Andrew de Lange to consider implications.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Organisations can be created/updated with a list of key value pairs in MetaDataSourcesInformation
    • This value can also be fetched with the appropriate endpoints
  • Sending a GHG data request from a Organisation that has Overseer as a key in MetaDataSourcesInformation will prepopulate the Overseer instructions in Provider with the value of in MetaDataSourcesInformation for Overseer.
    • After successfully connecting, it will also save the value of MetaDataSourcesInformation in the created connection parameters
  • Fetch is using the credentials set up for this newly created connection parameters

Title: [Mobble] Map Mobble data connector
Description:
Story
As a developer I want the relevant Mobble data mapped to Pure Farming resources so that I can implement the ingestion.

Description
The requirement is to map Mobble to the following resource types:
  • icarGroupMovementDepartureEventResource
  • icarGroupMovementDeathEventResource
  • icarGroupMovementBirthEventResource
  • icarGroupMovementArrivalEventResource
  • pfStockReconciliation
The plan (and the original mapping) was to ingest CSV files created from Mobble.  
However @Chris Hogg has established that the csv generator in Mobble generates the files in a JS function, so we can't download those files in an ingestion and parse them directly.

The document MobbleDemoFarmData.docx in the SharePoint Mappings>Mobble folder provides more background on:
  • accessing the demo farm
  • the Mobble reports and associated CSV files
  • the fields we can ingest into each resource type
  • some issues with Mobble, particularly that the farmers can rename or create their own names for stock classes.
More information about the Mobble data connector is available in the PF wiki.

Title: [Sainsburys] Genetic Codes
Description:
Hi both,

 

I’ve noticed that 30 SDDG farms don’t have any genetic codes in their FarmMetrics account. When I cross-referenced these with the list that Gamechanger sent over, it looks like their BL codes have been set up as another FM account, under the Non-SDDG package.

 

For example, 21_Lower Yetson (SDDG package) & 7042_Genus Supplier BL00009834 (Non-SDDG package).  SDDG - Missing Genetic Codes .xlsx

 

The Straw Data is being sent to PureFarming, so I’m assuming these SDDG farms will have data under the Non-SDDG unit, as this is where their genetic code is currently active.

 

Not sure how this data is processed, or how easily it may be to transfer from their Non-SDDG unit into their SDDG one? I also didn’t want to deactivate accounts or add in codes, for it to potentially cause errors.

 

Can you let me know what you would like the next steps to be?

 

Thanks,

 

Jess


TODO:
(setup test first and fix it in test first)
In SDDG - Missing Genetic Codes (Sheet1).csv you will find the following columns:
  • Unit ID - This refers to the unitId the Genus BL Code should be mapped to
  • Genus BL Code
  • Non SDDG Unit ID - This refers to the unitId the Genus BL Code is currently mapped to
  • PF HoldingID for BL Code - The HoldingId that has the connection for Genus BL Code in question
  • PF HoldingID for UnitId - The HoldingId that should have the connection for the Genus BL Code
Steps to resolve the issue:
  1. Delete connection for 'Genus BL Code' on 'PF HoldingID for BL Code'
  2. Re-Create all existing inventory transactions for that are currently on 'PF HoldingID for BL Code' to be on 'PF HoldingID for UnitId' as well.
  3. Mark all inventory transactions on 'PF HoldingID for BL Code' as deleted:
    1. set meta.is_deleted to True
    2. set updated_at to Now()
  4. Create new Genus connection on 'PF HoldingID for UnitId' for 'Genus BL Code'
    1. New Genus connections will be created when the automation is ready see #35224
Acceptance Criteria:
• There are no inventory transactions for any 'PF HoldingID for BL Code' that are not marked as deleted in PDS
  • The number of inventory transactions on 'PF HoldingID for UnitId' equals the number of soft deleted inventory transactions on 'PF HoldingID for BL Code'
  • No 'PF HoldingID for BL Code' has any 'active' Genus connection anymore
  • All 'PF HoldingID for UnitId' have the appropriate Genus connection with the 'Genus BL Code'

Title: [Sainsburys] Automate onboarding of FM PII files
Description:
We need a process (agreed with the FM team) to automate the ingestion of the kinermony beef files. 
REPO: moa-platform-service-onboarding/HoldingCreator at main · Pure-Farming/moa-platform-service-onboarding

Currently: 
  • Python scripts on bastion host
  • We receive a PII file (all FM unit information)
  • We run it manually
Where we want to be:
  • Automating the manual run
  • Extendable - anything with a PII file should be able to use it
    • Is this actually a requirement @Andrew Cooke? If so we will need to figure out how to know which customer this pii file belongs to based on the PII to know which grants to assign for those units/holdings (cc @Don Wilson)
Solution:
  1. Load PII file from drop-bucket
Proposed solution:
  • Listen to drop event
    • Drop Event
      •     s3_bucket_name = record["s3"]["bucket"]["name"]
            eventbridge_event = {
              "Source": f"moa.trigger.{data_source_name}",
              "Detail": json.dumps({
                "event_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
                "universal_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
                "triggered_at": str(datetime.utcnow()),
                "s3_event": {
                  "bucket_name": s3_bucket_name,
                  "object_key": s3_key
                }
              }),
              "DetailType": "trigger-event",
              "EventBusName": event_bus_name
            }
        see eu-west-1-drop-bucket-event-processor
  • Create a lambda function to run the script(s) and in drop the file to the s3 bucket. 
    • The script(s) in Bastion host are currently creating an onboarding yaml so we will need to drop the onboarding yaml from the lambda as well as it's resources (terms and logo) 
      • Solution use logo/term URLs instead of file references so those can be reused
  • Figure out how to keep a copy of the crated onboarding file for debugging purposes. Maybe with the preserveFile flag in onboarding (need to check if that is a thing and how it is called though)
The scope of this ticket is to only implement the 'create holding by pii file' script moa-platform-service-onboarding/HoldingCreator/src/create_holdings_from_pii_files.py at main · Pure-Farming/moa-platform-service-onboarding

Remove 'individual' field from pfGroupMovementBirthEvent
Description:
At some point we have introduced pfGroupMovementBirthEvent as an extension of icarGroupMovementBirthEventResource. To be more specific, the 'pf' version adds another attribute called individuals to the 'icar' version. 

Curently our implementation in the data processing pipeline is not consistent, in some places we are following the 'pf' version and in other places we follow the 'icar' version. It has now been decided that the individuals are not required, and we want to ditch the 'pf' version altogether (at some point). 

To achieve this we need to:
  • Remove the individuals column from pds+view+data writer.
  • Update the ingestion to refer to icarGroupMovementBirthEvent. (This should only be used by Trev, but worth double checking). Please double check that the individuals attribute is not populated by any data source (it was already checked, but just to be sure)
  • Remove pfGroupMovementBirthEvent from the developer hub. 
    • This basically means to remove it from our documentation repo (please get in touch with Don for more details) 
    • It also means we need to update the Resource Type Page to talk from icarGroupMovementBirthEvent instead of pfGroupMovementBirthEvent (this seems to be like that already....)

Background:
Currently Livestock group birth registration uses the icar type.
We want to change this in the output data api to use the pf type (https://developer.purefarming.com/documentation/schema/pfGroupMovementBirthEventResource.html)

This will add the field 'individuals' which is already exposed in the 'resource_type.livestock_group_birth_registration' view during #32012

The "individuals" field allows icarAnimalCoreResource records to be created at the same time as their births are recorded rather than a two-step process where the animals are created first, then the group birth movement is created.

NB: read the comments - intent of this story has changed.

Title: [GLFM] Add loggedOperation.products.components.quantity handling to PDS, data writer, Output Data Api
Tags: ADM; McCain; missed implementation; muddy boots
Description:
As a customer who is interested in the workRecordResource (and who pays for it) I would like to access all the attributes of the workRecordResource if provided by the farmer's choice of data source. This is in particular true for workRecordResource.loggedOperation.products.components.quantity

As it happens Muddyboots (GLFM) provides this exact field for spray activities however our database table (crop_work_record_logged_operations_products_components) does not contain the quantity field.



A customer like ADM, Weetabix or McCain might be interested in the quantity but we don't persist it in our database.

To resolve that we will need to 
  • Add the following columns for the quantity object to the `crop_work_record_logged_operations_products_components` table
    • Units - varchar/Enum
    • Measurement   - numeric
    • Resolution  - numeric
  • Add the same columns to the `crop_work_record` permissioned view
  • Update the data writer to write those three columns to the table
  • Update the output data API to consume the new columns of the permissioned view

Title: [Stock-Rec-Projection-Lambda] replace event bridge scheduler with fifo sqs
Description:
As everyone I want the projection lambda to work and not constantly be overwhelmed. :)

We remove the event bridge scheduler between collection lambda and the transformation lambda and replace it with a FIFO sqs queue (name: stock-rec-transformation.fifo), including dead letter queue (name: stock-rec-transformation-deadletter.fifo). Put messages for holding id in it with message deduplication id of holdingId (which sets the visibilityDelay to 5 mins)
  • We also need to update the collection lambda to put the message into the queue instead of the event bridge scheduler
  • We need to pass the queue name into the collection lambda
  • The transformation lambda needs to be triggered by the new queue instead of the scheduler
For testing:
Please try to test this with a lot of data so we can ensure that it does not get overwhelmed again with the new approach.

For Reference:
  • #22827
  • #22864
Acceptance Criteria:
• The collection lambda puts messages into the new queue (stock-rec-transformation-queue.fifo)
  • The transformation lambda gets triggered by messages in the stock-rec-transformation-queue.fifo

Remove PII from global DynamoDB table for entity service
Description:
Timebox to - 10 hours

The entity service currently may provide personally identifiable information for individuals, including:
  • Organisation name of sole traders
  • First and last names
  • Address (deprecated, not available in the UI, but may be/have been populated from Farm Metrics)
  • Telephone (deprecated, not available in the UI, but may be/have been populated from Farm Metrics)
This data is encrypted at rest and only ever delivered over encrypted connections, so meets our direct GDPR requirements, except that some data (address and telephone) is not required and therefore contradicts the Data Minimisation principle.

However, the presence of that data in DynamoDB tables that are synchronised across regions (Australia <-> EU/UK) may contradict the specific terms of agreements with customers. This is the same principle under which farm data is stored only within the relevant region.

Removing this would require either:
  • Recipients and Recipient Groups stored only in within-region DynamoDB tables, and the inclusion of Country or Region in a Data Request so that the correct data can be found on sign-up, or
  • Splitting out the specific PII fields and resolving these by reference when required (potentially impacting the performance of Discovery, for instance)
[Other suggestions welcome]

We almost certainly need a technical spike to understand scope of impact, potential and preferred solutions, and likely investment required to change this.

Title: [Rabo] 3. Automate processing of client list file (Add/Update Only)
Description:
When a new file is uploaded by Rabobank to the S3 bucket it should trigger a lambda function that loads the clients list into the system

Input: 
CSV file is encrypted:
  • Public Key cryptography
  • We generated the keypair, use secret key to decrypt
Encrypted CSV file with two headers - CUSTOMER_NAME, MDM Clients.GC_ID
From S3 upload file event:
S3 bucket: moa-platform-service-sftp-drop-prod-ap-southeast-2 (Look for SSM param - "/moa-platform-service-sftp/s3-bucket-name")
S3 Prefix: /rabobank/upload/*

Rename the SigningKey secret to PublicKey

Decrypt file using python-gnupg
  • Get keys from secrets (PrivateKey, PublicKey, Passphrase)
  • Need to import the keys into GPG
  • Decrypt CSV file before processing
  • Decrypt documentation
  • We need to install a dependency into the container
    • apt install gnupg2
Will need to setup some test data:
  • Create test CSV file
  • Create test GPG key, store in Secrets Manager
  • Encrypt the CSV file
  • Document in Wiki
For each customer:
  1. Check if the GCID exists as a recipient group (Internal Reference)
    1. If not - create the recipient group from the CUSTOMER_NAME
    2. If it is - update the recipient group's name from CUSTOMER_NAME
Assumption:
  • Repo is setup
  • Pipeline exists
  • Lambda exists and is triggered of S3 event
  • Internal reference/external reference work is already done
Output (From Lamba):
{
  added: [ GCID ],
  updated: [ GCID ],
}
Acceptance Criteria:
Should only process client-lists from Rabobank (/rabobank/upload/*)
Should add any missing orgs (recipient groups)
Should not duplicate any existing orgs (recipient groups)

There is a spike ( #36331 ) about a more generic approach to resolving this problem. To resolve the issue short-term for Sainsbury's, we need to stop writing PII data to our dynamo tables.

  • User: (please make sure that this applies to FarmUsers only, we do not want to update existing Sainsbury's Consumer users)

    • LastName: Contact
    • Email: anonymous_contact_<unit_id>@mapofag.com
  • Organisation: (please make sure that this applies to FarmOrganisations only, we do not want to update existing Sainsbury's Consumer Org)
    • Name: anonymous - Farm {farm_id}
    • Identifier: anonymous - Farm {farm_id}
  • Holding:

    • Name: anonymous - Unit {unit_id}
    • Address: NULL
    • City: NULL
    • PostCode: NULL
    • State: NULL
    • Identifier: anonymous - Unit {unit_id}
    • Reference: anonymous - Unit {unit_id}
    • FirstName  Anonymous 
    • LastName Contact
    • Email: anonymous_contact_<unit_id>@mapofag.com

Please make sure that the created 
  • Recipient's HoldingName, HoldingIdentifier, Reference and FarmBusinessName

are created without exposing any PII.

Addition:
Make sure the pii-holding-creator script uses non PII fields for identifying new units that get put into the created onboarding file.

Let's roll this out for Sainsbury's farmer data. Do not apply any of the changes to Consumer Org/Consumer Org users

Ingestion will trigger on schedule (Weekly)
  1. moa.trigger.mobble (name of source of trigger event)
  2. Authenticate with API
    1. Use refresh token to get new access token -> store new access and refresh token in encrypted data connection params
  3. Call the API and write a JSON file per call
    1. GET /organisation/{organisationId}/property/{propertyId}/report/range?start=<UTC TIMESTAMP>&end=<UTC TIMESTAMP>
      1. These calls should be batched into months (not including this month - last full month)
    2. GET /organisation/{organisationId}/property/{propertyId}/report/casualties?start=&end=
    3. GET /organisation/{organisationId}/property/{propertyId}/report/sales?start=&end=
    4. GET /organisation/{organisationId}/property/{propertyId}/report/purchases?start=&end=
    5. GET /organisation/{organisationId}/property/{propertyId}/report/increases?start=&end=
  4. Drop files into the raw bucket. Note the files must have the following metadata on them:
    1. mobbleOrganisationId
    2. mobblePropertyId
    3. startDateUtc
    4. endDateUtc
    5. mobbleApiEndpointName - casualties | sales | purchases | increases | range
      1. Optional if you want - include in file name
  5. Send fetch event to Standardise
API Docs: https://docs.apis.mobble.io/

NOTE ON UTC TIMESTAMP:
Get start of month in property timezone (data connection params) then convert to UTC before adding to URL

Initial run (initialRunComplete is false or not present in data connection params) retrieves 2 years of data from the current date

Subsequent runs should pull the last 15 months of data (to power the deletions)

Data Connection Parameters:
{
   "mobbleOrganisationId": "<GUID>",
   "mobblePropertyId": "<GUID>",
   "mobblePropertyTz": "<string>",
   "initialRunCompleted": <boolean | null?>
}
and the following encrypted params:
{
    "accessToken": "<string>",
    "refreshToken": "<string>"
}
Acceptance Criteria:
The files written to the raw bucket and the fetch event is published
  • Casualties
  • Sales
  • Purchases
  • Increases
  • 1 file per livestock reconciliation (range report) per month within the time range

Title: [Mobble] 3. Write ingestion - Standardise Livestock Reconciliations
Description:
As a consumer I want Mobble data standardised into a known format so that I don't have to deal with multiple data formats

Standardise Livestock reports from Mobble into Livestock Reconciliation events

Incoming Data Shape: https://docs.apis.mobble.io/#tag/livestock-reporting/GET/organisation/{organisationId}/property/{propertyId}/report/range

Mapping Doc: MapMobbleApiRangeReport.json
Additional Notes: https://mapofag.sharepoint.com/sites/Project/project_MapofAg_PureFarming/_layouts/15/doc.aspx?sourcedoc={a507c9a1-c9f9-47a9-84eb-d08529e0daa9}&action=edit

Data Point Key: https://dev.azure.com/rezaresystems/Map%20of%20Ag%20-%20New%20Product%20Development/_wiki/wikis/Pure%20Farming.wiki/928/Resource-Types?anchor=stock-reconciliation

Process for creating livestock reconciliations:
  1. Find all stock classes in the report (start, end, sales, casualties, purchases, increases)
  2. For each stock class, create a pf Stock Rec
    1. If no start value - set opening to 0
    2. If no end value - calculate closing (opening + increases + purchases - sales - casualties)
    3. If there is a discrepancy between closing number and calculated closing number adjust arrivals / departures (see mapping notes)
Acceptance Criteria:
Livestock Reconciliation event generated for the mobble Livestock report
Standardised event put onto regional event bus
livestock reconciliation change detection triggered
livestock reconciliation data-writer has no issues writing the data to the PDS

Description:
Sainsbury's have asked why they're not getting the sire breed, in the pregnancy check resource type, when they used to. Looking at the FM data exports, we are generating this, but were not passing it through Pure Farming.

            "Dam": "UK181475302900",

            "Insemination Date": "2024-03-11",

            "Estimated Outcome": "successful",

            "Likely Date of Birth": "2024-12-19",

            "Sire": "TRICKY",

            "Sire Breed": "HF",

            "Would be first offspring": "yes",

            "Is Eligible to Project": false


"Sire Breed" should be mapped to "predictedSireBreed.id" and "predictedSireBreed.scheme" should be set to "uk.cts.breed".
(See note in discussion May 28 or later on possible conversion of Sire Breed name to the code (or vice versa). )
"predictedSireBreedGroup" can be determined from "Sire Breed" by looking up in Breed Lookup.xlsx for a match in either Breed Name or Breed Code

Addition:
For AWS Cost reasons, we would want to avoid a historical data load to back populate existing Sire Breeds & Groups. The assumption is that there is a 1-1 relationship between Sire and Sire Breed. If so, we can just update existing rows in PDS with the according Breed/Group based on this relationship. We will need to verify, though, since the Sire comes with a name from FM, which means a name can be reused, so we want to record existing Sire/Beed relations. ( @Andrew de Lange FYI)
  • Record, Sire/Sire Breed, relations. 
  • It should look like 
    {
       "sire1": ["sire1 breed1", "sire1 breed2"],
       "sire2": ["sire2 breed1"]
    }
  • It should be done in Preprocess. And save in the preprocess bucket, similar to `farmmetrics/reasons/livestock/movement/animal-departure`, 
Also, @Rob Burgess is it possible to get an export from FM of existing Sire/Sire Breed relations?
Acceptance Criteria:
• Update Schema with the new predictedSireBreed and predictedSireBreedGroup fields
  • Update PDS table + view for the new predictedSireBreed and predictedSireBreedGroup fields
  • Update Pregnancy Check Data writer for the new fields
  • Update FM standardisation to map the new fields into the standardised data
  • Update Output data API to use the new version of the Schema and to map the new fields from the PDS View

Title: [Sainsburys] Same Genus reference can be linked with multiple unit ids
[Mobble] 4. Deletions
Description:
As a farmer and data consumer I want the data from Mobble to be accurate so that I can rely on it
  • If a farmer deletes a record it should be deleted in Pure Farming
Approach:
  • Pull in the last 15 months worth of data every time we call the Mobble API
  • Compare the retrieved data against the last retrieval of data (compare what we have in the standardised bucket from the previous day)
  • If a record is missing from the new API response AND it is in the timespan covered by the previous call
    • Assume the record is deleted - send deleted event (deletion lambda) to add a deleted flag to the data
    • Done via fire event to the change detection lambda 
This will apply to:
  • Sales (group departures)
  • Purchases (group arrivals)
  • Natural Increases (group births)
  • Casualties (group deaths)
  • Livestock Reconciliations (we may need to do 14 months instead of 15 as ending date for this is the end of the last full month)
key points:
  • Starting date = eventDateTime (for all other resources)
  • For Livestock Recon use startDate 
  • File needs to be matched on the data point key (to match the events)
  • e.g trev
Acceptance Criteria:
Deleting a record in Mobble will mean that the record is deleted in Pure Farming's PDS (soft delete)
Modifying a record in Mobble will mean that the original record in Pure Farming is deleted and a new record is created (within the same datapoint key)

Title: [Catalyst] 5.a. Update LLM Lambda to handle all input file types
Description:
The LLM Lambda currently only processes PDF files that are saved into the drop S3 bucket. We also need it to handle ZIP files containing one or more images.

An event handler step should be implemented so that, once a file from S3 is downloaded into a temporary location, it determines the file type and processes it accordingly:

  • If the file is a PDF: convert it to images (this is already implemented).
  • If the file is an Image: check format and convert if necessary
  • If the file is a ZIP:
    • Verify that all image files inside the ZIP are in a consistent format.
    • If the images are PNG or JPEG, convert them directly into byte arrays.
    • If the images are in HEIC format, convert them to JPEG first, then to byte arrays.

Title: [Catalyst] Update LLM to update the files tables for status and standardisation and end date
Description:
The last time I checked, the LLM prompt wasn’t generating output in the structure expected by the Standardisation class.

We need to make sure the LLM output is clearly defined and properly handled so that:

  • If the LLM fails to return any valid resource type data, the Lambda should update the job table directly with status "Error"
  • If the LLM successfully returns data, the Lambda should pass that data to the Standardisation class in the required format for further processing.
  • If the LLM successfully returns data, update the job table directly with status "Complete"
  • If the LLM successfully returns data, update the job table directly with end date utc now
  • Update job table with standardisation file URL
  • Give extraction Lambda access to the job table so that it can write to it (modify Lambda execution role)

Title: [FarmMetrics] Connection Timeout while fetching holding from Data Source Service
Tags: Entity Service; Prod Alert; Ready for dev; Sainsburys
Description:
Please note: this is not a critical issue.

We provide a read timeout when we send requests; this is set to 60 seconds by default. This is being exceeded sometimes when we attempt to fetch the holding for a given unitId for FarmMetrics. In my mind, there are three potential solutions:
  • Leave it as it is, since it retries and works normally in the second attempt
  • Increase the read timeout to 2 minutes. This would stop those messages spamming the prod alerts
  • Or we do it properly and fix the cause of the problem in the data source service and speed up the fetch. This could likely be a little bigger since I believe part of the problem is: not ideal secondary indexes on the data connection parameters dynamo table
  • Update Data Source Service: GET /connection/find-by-parameters endpoint to filter on DynamoDB for the value instead of filtering in code.
    • Currenlty the filter by value is happening here https://github.com/Pure-Farming/moa-platform-service-data-source/blob/ca193504d4b310e56a02da708a132e2a6a087f77/src/domain/MoA.Platform.DataSource.Domain.Services/Dynamo/DataConnectionService.cs#L177

    • This change will be applied across all endpoints users. In other words we don't change the endpoint signature to make filtering on dynamo optional, it will always filter on dynamo.

Title: (1) Specify additional parameters for a data request in JSON
Tags: Data Request Service; Discovery; Ecosystem API; Single Source
Description:
I want to add an "Additional parameters" string in data request so that we can provide the parameters as a single string, not multiple columns.

Follow how we have set up the additional parameters on data source object.

[DynamoDBProperty(typeof(GenericObjectConverter<Dictionary<string, string>>))]
public Dictionary<string, string>? AdditionalParameters { get; set; }

We also need to update the CRUD endpoints.

Background

Eventually it will make sense for applications that act on Data Requests (currently Provider and Appraise) to be able to take additional parameters. Here are some that we know about (not necessarily fully specified).

Provider: 
  • Data Source - means that once the user has accepted the request, they are taken directly to the connection UI for the specified data source, and completing that connection also completes the request (with any related redirection). 
Appraise:
  • Appraisal period - the start and end dates of the appraisal period. This is not necessarily the same as the date range that Appraise will use when it requests input data, because it might need data from outside the period.
  • Country - may or may not be needed; not just to do with the country of the holding, but also implies settings about what is displayed, how lists are filled, etc.
  • Model - which calculation model to use. Potentially this could be an array but we think one at present.
  • Survey - which SurveyJS survey loaded somewhere should be included for customer-specific data.
Potential solution
[To be edited after discussion]
  • Add an "Additional parameters" string in Discovery
  • MOA would provide our customers' admin person with the JSON to insert.
  • Add a string element to the Data Request to store this (as this can be opaque to Disco and ignored by Provider).
  • Ensure that implementers (Provider and Appraise) can get this data.
This doesn't include implementing the Data Source semantics in Provider - that is #35241
Acceptance Criteria:
data request service allows to add 'AdditionalParameters' to the DB. 
datarequest service, add and update endpoints. So POST data-request and PATCH data-request/{id}
New field is a JSON Object. "field" : {"key": "Value"}
data request works even if the new property is not used when creating a data request

Title: [Compliance with Terms and Agreements] 2. Blank/Anonymise data
Description:
As Sainsbury's, I want PF to align with the agreed terms. This means, in particular, that all data (including PII) related to Sainsbury's resides in the eu-west-1 region.

There is a spike ( #36331 ) about a more generic approach to resolving this problem. To resolve the issue short-term for Sainsbury's, we need to anonymise existing PII data. 

There are two types of anonymisation that we need to apply, depending on the data:
  • Update data with a string that doesn't allow traceback to the person
  • Blank the whole column
Some of the Sainsbury's data is already anonymised as we don't have all the information to make it different upon creation. But we can just overwrite those records to avoid complexity in the script to anonymise.

Task:
Columns to update:
  • Data Request Recipient: Accepted Email
    • desired anonymous email: anonymous_contact_<unit_id>@mapofag.com
      • Unit Id is the unit id that the user is admin of
  • Entity-Organisations: Name, Identifier
    • desired anonymous Name/Identifier: anonymous - Farm {farm_id} (the current naming is 'farmName - Farm {farm_id}' so the [farm_id} can be derived from the existing name)
  • Entity-Recipient-Groups: ContactEmail, ContactName, Identifier, Name
    • desired anonymous ContactEmail: anonymous_contact_<unit_id>@mapofag.com
    • desired anonymous ContactName: Anonymous Contact
    • desired anonymous Name/Identifier: anonymous - Farm {farm_id} ((the current naming is 'farmName - Farm {farm_id}' so the [farm_id} can be derived from the existing name)
  • Entity-Holdings: Identifier, Name:
    • desired anonymous Name/Identifier: anonymous - Unit {unit_id} (the current naming is 'unitName - Unit {unit_id}' so the [unit_id} can be derived from the existing name)
  • Entity-Users: Email, FirstName, LastName
    • desired anonymous email: anonymous_contact_<unit_id>@mapofag.com
    • desired anonymous firstName: Anonymous
    • desired anonymous lastName: Contact
  • Entity-Recipients: Email, FarmBusinessName, FirstName, LastName, HoldingIdentifier, HoldingName, Reference
    • desired anonymous email: anonymous_contact_<unit_id>@mapofag.com
    • desired anonymous farmBusinessname: anonymous - Farm {farm_id} (this used to be farmName only, so we need to keep the farm_id in memory somewhere)
    • desired anonymous firstName: Anonymous
    • desired anonymous lastName: Contact
    • desired anonymous holdingIdentifier, holdingName, reference: anonymous - Unit {unit_id} (the current holdingIdentifier is set to 'unitName - Unit {unit_id}' so the [unit_id} can be derived from the existing one)
Columns to blank: (should be done for everything, not just Saisnbury's related)
  • Entity-Holdings: Address1
  • Entity_Users: Address, ContactNumber
  • Entity-Recipients: Address
Addition:
  • Provide a spreadsheet with all the potential pii fields from Saiinsbury's so it can be checked by non devs as well again.
    • Ideally this would be done by a script so it can be rerun to verify the pii-creator-holding does not accidentally still add pii data

Scope:
Let's roll this out for Sainsbury's farmer data. Do not apply any of the changes to Consumer Org/Consumer Org users

FYI:  @Andrew Cooke @Chris Lloyd @Wade Parker @Miranda Hennebry
Acceptance Criteria:
• There is no PII data anymore for Sainsbury's related data
  • Spreadsheet with all pii related fields is attached to the story

Title: (2) [Ecosystem] Add a data source parameter to a data request

Assume data request service has been done.

Endpoint:

Body would be
{ "identifier": "string" }

Non-happy conditions
  • the logged in user must have control access on the data request's consumer organisation, return 404
  • the identifier does not exist, return 400
  • any of the existing recipient's country (derived from recipient group's country) does not match the data source's country, return 400
  • data sources that do not provide any of the requested resource types, return 400
  • data source does not cover all the requested resource types, return 400
Otherwise return 202.

The two 400 non-happy responses should come with a nice error message telling external developers what the issue is.

03 [Pii Onboarder] Update FM connection parameters to include pii customer, like Arla
Description:
As Arla/Sainsburys I want to ensure that only I can see unit data that belongs only to me.

The PII holding creator needs to add a new connection parameter to the FarmMetrics connection:
- The new parameter will be called `client` and will be set to the top-level folder in moa.ingestion.farmmetrics
  - For example: moa.ingestion.farmmetrics/arla/pii/... will result in connection parameters for this file to have `client` set to `arla`
- If there is no top-level folder, we shall set the client to `sainsburys`
- Update 'look-up' logic to check for units based on customer. So look up FarmMetrics connections based on `unit_id` as well as `client`

**Background:**
FarmMetrics has, generally speaking, multiple unit IDs for the same real-world farm. Each unit ID identifies a customer (like Arla) farm relationship. That is mostly true; one of the exceptions is Arla and Sainsbury's. Those share some unit IDs.

That means, for example, unitId 2 may be used in the context of Arla and Sainsbury's. Which in itself is not a problem, since we can permission Arla and Sainsbury's access to the same data. However, Sainsbury's has, for example, some additional monthly data that belongs to Sainsbury's only.

To bypass complex fetch logic to ensure Arla can't see Sainsbury's stats, we decided to create two separate holdings in PureFarming for unitId 2 (to stay within the example from above). 

To distinguish them, we want to add another attribute to the connection parameters called client. This will be populated based on the top-level folder where the pii file sits in. In case there is no top-level folder, we expect it to be Sainsbury's. (Sainsbury's data will move at some point to top-level Sainsbury's as well.)

So if a pii file gets dropped in: arla/pii/arla-pii-data.json every holding that is going to be created in that file will have a connection parameter called client, which is going to be populated with arla.

**Outlook:**
In another story, we are going to extend the FM ingestion to include the top-level folder for data ingestion as well. So if there is a top-level folder, we look for a holding with
- client: top-level-folder-name
- unit: unit-id of file
Acceptance Criteria:
- A pii file dropped in moa.ingestion.farmmetrics/pii creates onboarding files with FM connections that have `client` set to `sainsburys`
- A pii file dropped in moa.ingestion.farmmetrics/arla/pii creates onboarding files with FM connections that have `client` set to `arla`
  - And the same behaviour is true for any top level folder in `moa.ingestion.farmmetrics`
- Verify look up logic has been updated:
   - when checking for existing connections for consumer we take into account unit and client for existing connections
- Include the client name in the generated PII file name for ease of debugging later

Title: 02 [FarmMetrics] Update ingestion to find holdings by unitId and client
Description:
To ingest FarmMetrics data for the right holding, we will need to look up existing FarmMetrics connections by `unitId` and `client`.
  • At the moment, we get the unitId from the file's content. This can stay like that.
  • The client will be the top-level folder within `moa.ingestion.farmmetrics`
    • If there is no top-level folder, we assume the `client` to be `sainsburys`
Note:
For unknown clients, the behaviour will still be as it is right now for unit IDs that are not yet onboarded. The pre-processing step won't find any FM connections and exits. it somehow updates a missing units table, which will then send a list of missing units to the Penrith team. (The Penrith notification is only happening for Genus connections)

Background:
FarmMetrics has, generally speaking, multiple unit IDs for the same real-world farm. Each unit ID identifies a customer (like Arla) farm relationship. That is mostly true; one of the exceptions is Arla and Sainsbury's. Those share some unit IDs.

That means, for example, unitId 2 may be used in the context of Arla and Sainsbury's. Which in itself is not a problem, since we can permission Arla and Sainsbury's access to the same data. However, Sainsbury's has, for example, some additional monthly data that belongs to Sainsbury's only.

To bypass complex fetch logic to ensure Arla can't see Sainsbury's stats, we decided to create two separate holdings in PureFarming for unitId 2 (to stay within the example from above). 

When on board a new holding with the PII onboarder, we create an additional connection Parameter called client, which holds the name of the top-level folder where the PII data sits.

The actual raw data to be ingested will sit in the same folder. So need to look up holdings by unit id (from the file) and top-level folder. 

If there is no top-level folder, we assume it is Sainsbury's for now. And we don't have a client added.
Acceptance Criteria:
• FarmMetrics raw data dropped for a specific customer will be ingested for the customer's specific connection/holding.
  • If there is no top-level folder, the customer/client will be assumed to be Sainsbury's

Description:
Description

A lower-privileged user was able to access confidential data, including personal details, financial records, or system

credentials, either accidentally or intentionally, without proper authorization. This exposure could result in data

breaches, privacy violations, or the misuse of sensitive information.


Systems Affected

The following systems were affected:

• https://ap-southeast-2.amboss.test.purefarming.com/NotFound

• https://signin.test.purefarming.com/auth/realms/moa/account/account-security/linked-accounts


Impact and Severity

Exploiting the vulnerability lets users visit resources that are not allowed and may provide sensitive or confidential

information.


Recommendation

To remediate this finding:

• The application must validate authentication and prevent users from accessing resources when there is

no/invalid session.

• Ensure that access to sensitive data is strictly controlled based on user roles and responsibilities. Assign

permissions according to the principle of least privilege, granting users only the access necessary to perform

their job functions.


Reference

For more information on this finding:

• CWE-287: Improper Authentication - https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/287.html


CVSS

For the CVSS score calculation:

• https://www.first.org/cvss/calculator/3.1#CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N


Reproduction

To reproduce the issue, using a proxy such as Burp Suite:

• Visit the URL above and in the test environment we can see that testing information.

Figure 4 We can Testing Environment Information

security/linked-accounts' and verify the Keycloak dashboard is presented.

06 [Arla] Update PII onboarder to work for Arla only
Description:
As Arla, I want the PII onboarder to work, but as Sainsbury's I want it not to work

This is until the timeout issue ( #30062 ) is resolved.

We want to extend the PII holding Creator to set the 'ValidateOnly' Flag to True in case of Sainsbury's and to False in case of Arla.

This is done by a new environment variable called 'processing_blacklist'.

The processing_blacaklist variable holds a list of clients that we want ValidateOnly to be set to True. In case of environment variable VALIDATE_ONLY is set to true ALL clients have ValidateOnly set to True.
Acceptance Criteria:
• If the environment variable 'VALIDATE_ONLY ' is set to true, EVERY onboarding file for ANY client will have ValidateOnly set to True
  • If the environment variable 'VALIDATE_ONLY ' is set to false
    • If the current client of the run is in the Processing_Blacklist, the onboarding file has ValidateOnly set to True
    • If the current client of the run is NOT in the Processing_Blacklist, the onboarding file has ValidateOnly set to False

Title: [SAPUTO] Mapping CSV data
Description:
Story
As a developer I want a mapping of the Saputo interim CSV milk collection data to pfMilkCollection so that I can proceed with the data connectors.

Description
The mapping files can be found in the SharePoint folder Saputo.

This folder contains:
  • MapSaputoMilkCollection.json - the JSON mapping file
  • Mapping Notes.docx - some notes to explain the mapping
  • PickupLogic - workbook that has pickup examples that determine the PF production days and observation date
  • SampleSaputoMilkCollection.csv - a sample CSV data file provided by Saputo.  This is not real looking data.
  • SampleSaputoMilkCollectionPF.json - a PF JSON data file for a sample data record
The Mapping Notes are important.  They explain how to determine PF's "productionDays" from Saputo's Date, PickupSequence and PickupType.  Also the convention assumed for "observationDate".

common class exist to convert DIF format file to CSV/JSON

Title: [Recipient Holding][Entity] 1. TODO: Manually fix multiple-holdings on a recipient
Description:
As a developer I need the data in a consistent state before completing the lock-holdings work so that data does not end up in a bad state.

Chris L ran the following export from Pure Farming which contains a list of all recipients and their respective holdings (filtered to ONLY the recipients with multiple holdings). Sarah in the UK has been going through them and seeing what we would need to do:

prod_recipients_with_multiple_holdings.xlsx

Notes:
  • GATEKEEPER - we don't ingest any data from GateKeeper, so we can safely move the data connection between holdings
  • RPA - will have data ingested against a holding, do not modify these holdings
  • MUDDYBOOTS - Telus Farm Managment - will have data ingested, do not modify these holding

This ticket: clean up all existing connections (in the spreadsheet above, and also search for any more recipients with multiple holdings attached after that)

Moving a data-connection over to a new holding (e.g. Gatekeeper), no data
  • Update the holdingId & organisationId in the data connection
    • Rather delete and re create instead (so we can use APIs)

Tidy up / remove old holding and data-request-recipient:
  • Soft delete the holding
  • Move data
    • Find data with old entity id, replace with new entity id
    • Only top-level tables (what are the top level tables for resource type)
  • Grants - verify the grants on the old holding cover the same themes as the new holding
    • If not - create new grants (probably not needed)
    • If they do all good
    • Revoke grants on old holding
  • Update the holdingId on the data-request-recipient

Potential errors: This spike is to look into them #36627
  • Updating a data-request recipient only works when the DRR is in a particular state
  • Emails on DRRs (which one do we use)
  • If a recipient's email is updated
    • When the recipient gets a new data request
      • Does it create a new organisation? If so how do we give this permission to the existing holding (for locking recipient)
    • Possible change to signup required?
      • At the moment you can use the same signup link over and over again IF the data request is PENDING
      • Potentially could use a new status "SENT" or "OPENEND" to stop this? (try to make the changes to signup only to avoid extra provider work)

Title: [Provider] Make complete request more irresistibly clickable

  • Arrivals can be from dairy ("dairy-monthly-data"), non-sddg or beef ("integrated-beef") files
Acceptance Criteria:
• If an animal (with an arrival as method ["on"]) is removed from yesterday's raw file we see a deleted arrival
  • If an animal (with an arrival as method ["on"]) is changed to a birth from yesterday's raw file we see a deleted arrival
  • If an animal's (with an arrival as method ["on"]) arrival date changes from yesterday's raw file we see a deleted arrival for yesterday's arrival date
  • If the previous standardisation run was exactly 60 days ago and one of the above condition is met, we should still see a deletion
    • To set this scenario up you can change the standardisation folder's date to 60 days ago.
  • If the previous standardisation run was earlier than 60 days ago we should NOT see a deletion, even if any of the above contions is met.

Title: [Aus Dairy DIF] UI file upload
Description:
As a Farmer I want to be able to provide my Easy Dairy data to a data consumer so that they can receive my heard movement and observation

Bey has provided the mocks in figma here.


Note: BFF changes has been made in Easy Dairy that allows it to be used for Australian Dairy DIF export with minimal changes. 

Scope:
  • We need to create a UI in Provider for a new data source named Australian Dairy DIF.
  • In Provider, farmers will upload a Zip file which contains files using the DIF data format. 
  • Make the file upload part a reusable component
  • UI should only allow ZIP file 
  • Un-zip and validate the files 
    • content of file is 194 chars per row (Check first row only)
    • first 3 chars of row is "102"
    • has at least 1 row
  • This file will need to be save to the Drop Bucket via BFF
    • moa.ingestion.easydairy/{date} yyyy-mm-dd
    • file name e.g.: (use whatever is available) u_{universalId}_e_{entityId}
Instructions:

Import data using DIF file

Step 1: Export the File

  1. On your farm software or system, export the file using the “102 Cow Pedigree Records” Format.
  2. Make sure the file has a .102 extension.
  3. Zip (compress) the file.
  4. Come back to this page and upload the zipped file.

Step 2: Upload your file(s) below

  1. Click “Choose file”.
  2. Select the zip file you just downloaded/created.
  3. Click “Confirm connection” to complete.
Acceptance Criteria: Additionally, regression test Easy Dairy UI Upload as shared code was modified.

Title: Add Encrypted Connection Parameters for Data Connections
Description:
Add a new, nullable field to data connections in the data-source service.

Field Name: encryptedParameters
Type: String

This field will store ciphertext. The ciphertext is a JSON Blob with connection parameters (the same as the connectionParameters field) which has been encrypted with the KMS key

_________ // TODO put KMS key info here

Essentially this will mean that we have the following:

GET /data-source/connection/{id} 
{
  "dataConnection": {
    "id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
    "farmId": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
    "organisationId": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
    "enterpriseId": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
    "createdBy": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
    "createdDate": "2023-07-03T01:04:09.974Z",
    "dataSourceTermsAcceptedAt": "2023-07-03T01:04:09.974Z",
    "connectionParameters": {
      "myParameter": "myValue"
    },
    "encryptedConnectionParameters": "tWfbZaxMBYGlJQpPT3hDEnFErovLgtrguQ0HLm0I29ttkQ+7o2tUlStlF9s7SJaA",
    "awsRegion": "string"
  }
}

Notice the "encryptedConnectionParameters" field which contains a string. When the string is decrypted with the KMS key it produces:

> decrypt tWfbZaxMBYGlJQpPT3hDEnFErovLgtrguQ0HLm0I29ttkQ+7o2tUlStlF9s7SJaA
{
    "my_encrypted_param": "my_value"
}

The above is not a real command, just a demonstration. 
The idea is that the Provider BFF will create the JSON blob, encrypt it and send the encrypted string when it creates the data connection.

The Provider BFF does not have the ability to decrypt "encryptedConnectionParameters"
The Ingestions only have the ability to decrypt the "encryptedConnectionParameters"

Generate stock reconciliations 2: Initialise holding
Description:
Story
I want to initialise the generation of a holding's stock reconciliations so that the counting of animal movements for groups x month can begin.

Description
Clear from memory all the animal details stored for the previous holding that was processed.
Zero the counts for all groups x months.

Note that groups are birthPeriod-sex groups (e.g. "2022-07-01/2022-12-31" & "Female"
 and months are calendar year-months (e.g. "2023-05").

For each of these groups x month there will be separate counts for:
  • births
  • arrivals
  • departures
  • deaths.
For example, essentially we may have numberBirths ( "2022-07-01/2022-12-31", "Female", "2023-05").

See note in #22855 about a suggested indexing of these birthPeriod-sex group labels and calendar year-month labels.
Acceptance Criteria:
After the memory storage for the holding has been initialised, there should be:
  • no animal details stored for the holding
  • the elements of the arrays of counts for births, arrivals, departures and deaths should all have the value zero.
  • the elements of the array of counts for onHand should all have the value zero.

Title: Live Matrix FM Data Connector
Eco-Add Recipient(s) to List
[Ecosystem] Modify one or more Recipients
Description:
As a data consumer I want to be able to modify one or more recipients through the ecosystem API so I can programmatically update a recipient


PUT /ecosystem/organisations/{OrganisationId}/recipients

Parameters to be passed -

An array of recipients

Reference - String, mandatory (must specify RecipientId or Reference)

RecipientId - String, mandatory (must specify RecipientId or Reference)

First Name - String, mandatory

Last Name - String, mandatory

Address - String, mandatory

Email - String, mandatory

Farm Business Name - String, mandatory,

Holding Name - String, mandatory



Response -

200 when the recipient object in the list is updated successfully with the new recipient details 

Return list of failed recipient IDs with error message(e.g invalid recipient ID or mandatory fields missing)

and list of successful recipient objects 

{

 "successes": [ recipient1, recipient2, ... ],

 "failures": [ { recipient: recipient3, reason: string }, ... ]

} 


Exceptions -

Not authenticated or  - 401

No access to org - 403

No values provided in the API parameters - 400
Acceptance Criteria:
If the user does not have permission over the organisation - returns 403
If the user is not authenticated - returns 401
If not all parameters are specified in the POST body - 400 (with helpful error message)
If recipient successfully updated it is returned in the successes list
If the recipient did not update successfully it is returned in the failures list with a reason why it failed
Either the reference or the recipientId must be specified, but not both - this would cause a recipient to end up in the "failures" list in the response

      "emailAddress": "john.smith@example.com",
      "organisationName": "John's Farm"
    }
  2. Redirect to signup.purefarming.com/third-party/start/details?orgId={organisationId}&details={base64encodedJson}
    Where JSON is as above, but Base64 encoded for transport and to make it opaque.
  3. Add an endpoint to the Ecosystem API, POST api.purefarming.com/ecosystem/application/user/signup
    With JSON Body:
    {
      "organisationId": "{organisationId}",
      "userDetails": {
        "firstName": "John",
        "lastName": "Smith",
        "emailAddress": "john.smith@example.com",
        "organisationName": "John's Farm"
      }
    }
    Response:
    {
      "organisationId": "{organisationId}",
      "url": "https://signup.purefarming.com/third-party/start?instance={instanceId}"
    }
Preference Order (complexity): 2, 1, 3

The initial endpoint shall:
  1. Decode and parse the JSON
  2. Save the details so that they can be shown on the appropriate screens
  3. Redirect to signup.purefarming.com/third-party/start?orgId={organisationId}

Potential Issues:
  1. Angular not able to accept POST requests, and/or does not have access to the POST body
  2. Base64 encoding is determined to be not secure enough, therefore we need to look at other alternatives

Title: /milk-statement- Permissioned Database Views
Description:
This allows the output api to function. It is a view of the whole resource type in one view query, with the permissions applied, so a user can only see data for holdings they are permitted to access.
Repository: moa-platform-primary-datastore

  1. Add a migration (see README in PDS repo)
  2. Create a SQL file in MoA.Platform.Persistance > Sql
    Note: Ensure the file is an embedded resource
  3. Run the file in the migration by
    1. Making the migration inherit from "SqlMigration" instead of "Migration"
    2. Running the script by using "EmbeddedResource(NAMESPACE_OF_FILE, migrationBuilder)"
  4. Create a view that joins all the resource type tables together, then applies permissions for the user
    1. Include table prefix on all columns returned (e.g. the 'cwr' in 'cwr_id' for Crop Work Record ID)
    2. Ensure user permissions are passed in by joining to the permissions.my_holding_access view (see other resource type views)
    3. The view created should be called "your_resource_type" and should be created in the "resource_type" schema
  5. Scaffold the database objects (see README)
  6. Add in any enums / enum columns (see README)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Migration runs without error
  • All columns prefixed with table short code (e.g. cwr_)
  • User permissions applied

  • In the 'build/azure-pipelines.yml' file
    • Change the folder in the trigger > paths > include section and pr > paths > include section
    • Change the project name in the variables section
    • Update the rootDirectory and buildDirectory folder paths in the variables section
    • Replace 'Resource-Type=xxxx' wherever it appears
    • Update the 'RESOURCE_TYPE_PATH' wherever it appears
    • In the dev/test deployment sections update the ResourceTypeSafeName, ResourceTypeName, and ResourceTypeModuleName variables
  • In the 'build/deployment-stage-template.yml' file
    • Update the ResourceTypeName, ResourceTypeSafeName, and ResourceTypeModuleName variables
    • Update the 'resourcetypetag' in the parameters section
  • Clear out the 'lambdas/ChangeDetection/main.py' code
  • Clear out the 'lambdas/DataWriter/main.py' code
  • Clear out the 'tests/*.py' files
  • Clear out any sample files in the 'lambdas/DataGenerator' folder
  • In Azure DevOps create the pipeline and make sure it's saved in the pipeline folder

Sample Data Generator:
Add the resource type to the data generator and into dev, test, and prod
  • Create a dummy data generator that provides sample data. This allows all the other parts of the processing to be developed and tested.

    See an example here: https://github.com/Pure-Farming/moa-platform-data-processing/tree/main/resource_types/crop/work_record/lambdas/DataGenerator 
  • The lambda function should put an event onto eventbridge. Most of this code is handled for you by the higher-level classes.
    See the wiki page below about how to run the data generator:
  •   https://dev.azure.com/rezaresystems/Map%20of%20Ag%20-%20New%20Product%20Development/_wiki/wikis/Pure%20Farming.wiki/686/Sample-Data-Generator

  • In the data-generator (moa-environment-demo repo)

  • Add SQL statements, methods to upsert data into the primary datastore

# Resource Type: Crops – Work Record

- [URLs](#urls)
- [Response Structure](#response-structure)

## URLs

Get all work records that you have access to.

```
GET /data/crops/work-records
```

Get an individual work record, regardless of its holding
---

## Response Structure

A call to the Work Record endpoints returns the following fields:

```json
{
    "id": "string",
    "loggedOperations":[ … ]
}
```

| Response Item | Description | Data Type |
| ------------- | ----------- | --------- |
| **Id** |The Pure Farming Id of this Work Record |UUID |
| **Logged Operations** | Array of the operations completed in this work record | Array of [Operations](/resource-types/crops/work-record.md#operations)
---

## Operations

This represents an individual operation within the work record

```json
{
    "id": "string",
    "operationName":"string"
}
```

| Response Item | Description | Data Type |
| ------------- | ----------- | --------- |
| **Id** |The Pure Farming Id of this Operation Record |UUID |
| **Operation Name** | Name of the field operation | String

    Recommendation: Create a SQL file, use the diagram code from above and tweak it, create a new EF migration
  3. Ensure you update the database models and add any enum fields (see README in moa-platform-primary-datastore project)

Database View Steps:
  1. Add a migration (see README in PDS repo)
  2. Create a SQL file in MoA.Platform.Persistance > Sql
    Note: Ensure the file is an embedded resource
  3. Run the file in the migration by
    1. Making the migration inherit from "SqlMigration" instead of "Migration"
    2. Running the script by using "EmbeddedResource(NAMESPACE_OF_FILE, migrationBuilder)"
  4. Create a view that joins all the resource type tables together, then applies permissions for the user
    1. Include table prefix on all columns returned (e.g. the 'cwr' in 'cwr_id' for Crop Work Record ID)
    2. Ensure user permissions are passed in by joining to the permissions.my_holding_access view (see other resource type views)
    3. The view created should be called "your_resource_type" and should be created in the "resource_type" schema
  5. Scaffold the database objects (see README)
  6. Add in any enums / enum columns (see README)
Acceptance Criteria:
Database Schema:
  • Migration exists that creates the tables in the primary datastore
  • References between tables are done using bigserial/bigint not uuid
    (Note: refencing entities should be done using uuid)
  • EF Models are updated
  • Enums / enum columns have been manually added as per README
  • Migration runs
Database View:
  • Migration runs without error
  • All columns prefixed with table short code (e.g. cwr_)
  • User permissions applies

Description:
Repo: https://github.com/Pure-Farming/Documentation 

API documentation of the output data api is in devhub. This involves:
  1. Creating a markdown file in the Documentation repo
    1. Header
      The metadata of the page (title, menu_order, post_status, post_excerpt, taxonomy). See one of the other pages as an example
    2. Introduction
      The Resource Type Name with a list of sections in the document (URLs, Response Structure)
    3. URLs
      The available URLs to do with this resource type
    4. Response Structure
      The response objects, with a description of the different fields

This markdown file is interpreted and displayed using Redoc - https://redocly.com/docs/

Example / basic skeleton: 
---

title: Work Record
menu_order: 1
post_status: publish
post_excerpt: Crops - Work Record
taxonomy:
    category:
        - api
        - data
    post_tag:
        - api

---

# Resource Type: Crops – Work Record

- [URLs](#urls)
- [Response Structure](#response-structure)

## URLs

Get all work records that you have access to.

```
GET /data/crops/work-records
```

Get an individual work record, regardless of its holding
---

## Response Structure

A call to the Work Record endpoints returns the following fields:

```json
{
    "id": "string",
    "loggedOperations":[ … ]
}
```

| Response Item | Description | Data Type |
| ------------- | ----------- | --------- |
| **Id** |The Pure Farming Id of this Work Record |UUID |
| **Logged Operations** | Array of the operations completed in this work record | Array of [Operations](/resource-types/crops/work-record.md#operations)
---

## Operations

This represents an individual operation within the work record

```json
{
    "id": "string",
    "operationName":"string"
}
```

| Response Item | Description | Data Type |
| ------------- | ----------- | --------- |
| **Id** |The Pure Farming Id of this Operation Record |UUID |
| **Operation Name** | Name of the field operation | String

Description:
This involves inspecting the primary datastore (or eventually the event stream) and determining whether the incoming data represents a change of state of the overall system or not. It must check field by field for equivalence (that the new data being written would not cause a change) - this is not quite the same as checking that it exactly matches.

MLA Fusion: Livestock age class model - data mapping
Description:
A  set of aggregation functions (triggered in some way to be decided by Daniel) to do the stock reconciliation calculations as follows:
  • Potentially create an Animal Group that describes the age and sex combinations of animals, and number of animals -- or we may just decide to use the animal sets (they may lack sex and age).
  • Aggregate individual animal movement resources for individuals with the same age and sex classes and/or Animal Set into Group movement resources with the number of animals involved. 
  • Process over each Animal Group and aggregate movement records into (say) monthly totals, each of which gets put into a Stock Reconciliation resource (a stock rec resource for a month and an animal group just has an opening and closing number, and the totals of arrivals, births, deaths, departures).
  • The typical method of generating these is to start with a known state (often the present, if the number of animals in a group represents present state) and then work back, adding death/departure totals, and subtracting birth/arrival totals to get the opening number, then making that the closing number for the previous month, and so on.

Title: As a Discovery User I want to delete a holding (recipient) with data
Description:
As a Discovery User with control access, I want to delete a holding from the list of holdings in the organisation

Business Case:
Customers are charged per holding with data connected, so they need to be able to remove it if they are no longer receiving data

Scenarios we need to think about:
  1. I made a mistake loading it and want to delete it from the list
  2. GDPR requirements - someone is asking us to delete it (may need to be deleted across multiple organisations)
  3. I no longer want to receive data from this holding
  4. Remove the holding from a data request
If a holding / recipient is (soft) deleted:
  1. Revoke grants - need to think about delegated authority (find all grants where the resource is the holding and the grantee is the organisation that I'm currently in, delete those grants)
  2. PII gets removed, but ID stays - to include traceability 
  3. deletedat column updated (include timestamp)
  4. deletedby column updated - logged in user
  5. The data will stay in PF
  6. Email the provider to notify of deletion - new template (xx will no longer be taking your xxx data)
  7. If no data (or request rejected), just ask to delete, if there is a data request that has been accepted then a popup appears to confirm deletion
  8. If there is a pending request and it is deleted, then the provider should be notified (cancel the data request) - 1 email in total - don't email, but if they click on the link in the email, it will have expired
  9. Once the holding has been deleted, the list gets updated to show the holding is no longer there
  10. Trigger a recalculation of the authorisation cache for the grants that were removed
  11. If you delete multiple holdings, you will be prompted if there are any with an accepted data request and send an email to the provider for those holdings - still get a prompt even if there are no accepted data requests with any of the holdings

If the deleted recipient is re-added to discovery then it is detected as existing already and details are updated

https://www.figma.com/proto/40RX5U7cDDnDbTYarhexRk/PF-Discovery-Prototype?node-id=4432%3A17629&starting-point-node-id=4334%3A11502&scaling=scale-down
Acceptance Criteria:
Given I am a Discovery user
If I have control access to the organisation
Then I am able to delete a holding

Given I am a Discovery user
If I do not have control access to the organisation
Then I am not able to delete a holding

Given I am a Discovery user
When I delete a holding
Then the list is refreshed to show the holdings is not there anymore

Given I am a Discovery user
When one or more grants are revoked
Then I can no longer access data through the output API - may not be instantaneous

The data request recipient is marked as deleted, deletedat and deletedby is added (data request service)
The recipient itself is marked as deleted, deletedat and deletedby is added (entity service)
Deleted data request recipients should not be as part of the data request
If you query for a deleted recipient, the API should return a 404

Given I am a Discovery user
When a holding is deleted and there is an accepted data request
Then the provider will receive an email telling them it has been removed

Title: Create Permissioned View for Query Get Sample Plans in Database in Primary Data Store
Description:
We already have the query to get data, we just need to apply the permissions to it.
  1. Take the existing query that we have and turn it into a view that has the permissions applied to it
  2. Modify the existing holding/id/crop/sample-plans endpoint to use the new view which then allows us to test it
  3. Add a new endpoint which is crops/sample-plans
The new schema is called resource_type

--------------------------------------------


CURRENT QUERY I'M WORKING WITH: 
-- This should get all the DIRECT resource permissions

SELECT * 
FROM permissions.grants
LEFT JOIN permissions.granted_actions as actions
    ON grants.id = actions.grant_id
LEFT JOIN permissions.granted_themes as themes
    ON grants.id = themes.grant_id
INNER JOIN permissions.resource_type_themes as resource_type_themes
    ON theme.id = resource_type_themes.theme_id
INNER JOIN internal.resource_types as resource_types
    ON resource_type_themes.resource_type_id = resource_types.resource_type_id
WHERE
    grants.grantee_id = '25aabeb6-e7b7-4c01-ab70-dab57ebf02bb'
    AND resource_type.short_name = '/core/sample-plan'
    AND (
        themes.action IN ('read', 'write', 'control') OR
        actions.action IN ('read', 'write', 'control')
    )

[File Service] Get Single File Metadata
Description:
As a farmer I want to get information on a single file so that I can get information on just this file without having to get information on all files.

Return latest version if not specified
Must be done first: #32434 

GET /files/{file id}/{version_number:optional}

Query Filters:
?deleted=true|false

Response:
200 OK, 404 Not Found,  

    {
      "owningEntity": "GUID",
      "owningEntityType": "string",
      "name": "string",
      "extension": "string",
      "virusState": "CLEAN, PENDING, DANGER",
      "sourceService": "string",
      "uploadedAt": "datetime",
      "version": int, (defaults to latest)
      "id": "GUID",
      "public": bool,
      "metadata": [
          { "key": "string": "value": string}
      ],
      "objectUrl10Min": "pre-signed S3 url",
      "deletedAt": "datetime"
    }
Grants:
  • Must have control or read over the owningEntity
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass in file Id (incl version)
  • Get the metadata for a file
  • Gets the latest version if no version is specified
  • Defaults to deleted=false
  • Specifying a version number returns the specific version (or 404 if the version doesn't exist)
  • 404 returned if the user doesn't have access to the file
