Title: [Rabobank] Hide Clients in Disco
[eNVD] Handle Deletions (Group Arrivals)
Description:
We have already done deletions for Group Departures, need to do it for Group Arrivals.

When records are deleted from eNVD they need to be removed from Pure Farming.

Deleted records are no longer returned to the API
  • Using new deletion lambda
    • Take standardised data
    • Look back at the standardised data
      • Compare previous standardised data
      • Look for items that used to be present that are no longer present
    • Generates new "deleted" standardised file
      • Returns just the missing data with "isDeleted" set in the metadata
    • Triggers change detection for new "deleted" standardised file
Doing this for ALL eNVD resource types
  • Group Arrival
How far are we looking back?
  • 1 year
Acceptance Criteria: When API Response from eNVD is missing a Group Arrival record within 1 year that was previously there it is deleted from the Primary Datastore

Title: /livestock/animal-group Real Change Detection
Description:
This involves inspecting the primary datastore (or eventually the event stream) and determining whether the incoming data represents a change of state of the overall system or not. It must check field by field for equivalence (that the new data being written would not cause a change) - this is not quite the same as checking that it exactly matches.

Repository: moa-platform-data-processing
File: /resource_types/livestock/animal-group/lambdas/ChangeDetection/main.py
Acceptance Criteria:
• If an object exists in the pds, and it isn't a change, it is not loaded onto the data-writer queue
  • If an object exists in the pds, and it IS a change, it is loaded onto the data-writer queue
  • If an object does not exist in the pds, it is loaded onto the data-writer queue

    lap.gender AS lap_gender,

    la.meta_processed_at AS la_meta_processed_at,
    la.location_identifier_id AS la_location_identifier_id,
    la.location_identifier_scheme AS la_location_identifier_scheme,
    labr.animal_identifier_id AS labr_animal_identifier_id,

   		(select mhanm.holding_id from permissions.my_holding_access_materialised mhanm where

     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal la ON la.animal_id = labr.animal_id
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal_alternative_identifiers laai ON laai.animal_id = la.animal_id
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal_breed_fractions labf ON labf.animal_id = laai.animal_id
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal_parentage lap ON lap.animal_id = labf.animal_id;

    labr.created_at AS labr_created_at,
    labr.updated_at AS labr_updated_at,
    labr.meta_source_kind AS labr_meta_source_kind,
    labr.meta_source_id AS labr_meta_source_id,
    labr.meta_ingested_at AS labr_meta_ingested_at,
    labr.meta_processed_at AS labr_meta_processed_at,
    labr.location_identifier_id AS labr_location_identifier_id,
    labr.location_identifier_scheme AS labr_location_identifier_scheme,
    labr.meta_is_deleted AS labr_meta_is_deleted,
    labr.pf_metadata AS labr_pf_metadata
   FROM
   		(select mhanm.holding_id from permissions.my_holding_access_materialised mhanm where 
   			mhanm.resource_type_short_name in ('*', '/livestock/movement/animal-birth-registration') 
     		 and mhanm.user_sub::text = current_setting('current.sub'::text)::character varying::text
     	) perms
     	join  data.livestock_animal_birth_registration labr on labr.entity_id = perms.holding_id 
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal la ON la.animal_id = labr.animal_id
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal_alternative_identifiers laai ON laai.animal_id = la.animal_id
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal_breed_fractions labf ON labf.animal_id = laai.animal_id
     LEFT JOIN data.livestock_animal_parentage lap ON lap.animal_id = labf.animal_id;

Title: Sprint 0 Activities
Description:
As a purefarming developer I want to have an environment created so that I can easily use the enterprise data connector
  1. Setup infrastructure
  2. Setup API
  3. Setup database table
  4. Setup and configure swagger
  5. Setup pipeline
  6. Setup sonar
  7. Create the S3 bucket and make sure the database IAM role has access to write to the bucket, and our API IAM role that has read and list access to the bucket
Acceptance Criteria: You can deploy an API with a test endpoint that returns "true" to an environment

Title: Create request endpoint
Description:
As a data consumer developer I want to be able to request an export of data from PureFarming so that I can import it
  1. A post endpoint taking in object:
    1. reference
    2. list of resource types
    3. requesting AWS account reference
    4. mode (1 of dump or stream)
    5. type (CSV, TSV)
  2. Returns an individual request response object
  3. Return 400 if reference is not unique for this consumer

Title: Get an individual export
Description:
As a data consumer developer I want to get an individual export so that I can see its status
  1. If its not found, return 404
  2. If it is found, copy the list of properties from the list endpoint
  3. If I don't have access to the export, return 404
  4. Individual data request response
Query Parameters:
  1. ID or Reference (unique to data consumer)

Title: Create reference endpoint
Description:
As a data consumer developer I want to see what resource types are available so that I see what data I can request
  1. Returns a list of resource types containing:
    1. name
    2. link to the output data api endpoint
    3. count of records that I have access to
    4. last updated (date record was last updated that I have access to)

Title: Trigger data export
Description:
As a data consumer I want the export task to be triggered when I request an export so that I can receive the data
  1. The API should put an item on the queue to be processed
  2. As jobs get finished new jobs should be picked off the queue
  3. Don't know whether this is an ECS tasks or a lambda because we won't know how long the export takes
  4. Daniel has written the code to do the export
    1. call it multiple times for multiple resource types
    2. 1 request on the queue for each resource type
    3. uses the native s3 functions in RDS to stream data to the bucket

Title: Generate pre-signed URLs
Description:
As a data consumer developer I want to receive pre-signed download URLs for my exported files so that I can import them into my environment
  1. If a requesting AWS account is provided, the download links should be created as the requestor pays so that MOA does not get charged for the data transfer
  2. If no AWS ID is provided the standard pre-signed URL is created
  3. Pre-signed URLs should be valid for 5 mins
  4. Pre-signed URLs should only be generated for export files that exist
We can use the drop bucket as a reference. It has a similar feature.

Description:
SHOULD THIS BE TRIGGERED FROM THE HOLDING DELETE MESSAGE ON EVENTBRIDGE?

We're going to call this the PDS Duplicate Deletion Tool
  • Lambda Function that...
    • Runs a SQL script that 
      • Finds all duplicate datapointkey-holding records
      • Deletes as many as required to get the latest record
    • Update record in change-detection dynamo table

{

    "changeDetectionTable": "eu-west-1-change-detection-animal",
    "pdsTableName": "livestock_animal"
  },
  "db_user_secret": "/pds/user/proc/abc"
}

Architecture:


New IAM role needs permission
  • lambda execution
  • get secrets for /pds/user/proc/*
  • update row from dynamodb where change-detection-*
Create a tooling repo to store this in 
  • Future TODO: move unboarding tool
Acceptance Criteria:
• New Git Repo created: "moa-platform-tools"
  • Lambda runs and:
    • removes all duplicates from pds for a given resource type
    • sets all records in dynamodb for duplicate data point key-holding combos to {}
    • outputs list of duplicate keys found
      (return list_of_duplicates)

Title: Remove Lists - /ecosystem/organisations/{orgId}/data-requests
Description:
Modify the Ecosystem API /ecosystem/organisations/{OrganisationId}/lists/{ListId}/data-requests endpoint

At the moment this method is removed from the controller, but it's time to bring it back in a new form...
New Path: /ecosystem/organisations/{OrganisationId}/data-requests (remove lists/{ListId})
New Incoming Object: 
{
  "purpose": "Access to vineyard sampling data to analyse historical trends.",
  "subject": "Data request to access your vineyard sampling data",
  "message": "Hi, we would like your data please.",
  "recipients": [
    {
      "reference": "FarmMeadow-JohnSmith",
      "email": "john.smith@farmmeadow.co.nz",
      "firstName": "John",
      "lastName": "Smith",
      "address": "3684 Farm Meadow Drive, RD3, NZ",
      "farmBusinessName": "Farm Meadow",
      "holdingName": "Farm Meadow Merlot",
      "holdingIdentifier": "FM-324"
    }
  ],
  "terms": [
    {
      "termsId": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
      "documentUri": "string"
    }
  ],
  "themes": [
    "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"
  ]
}
(now includes list of recipients)

Notes (Validation/Errors): 
  • Recipients MUST have have a Reference, optionally may specify all other recipient data
  • If just reference is specified, find recipient. 
    • If found then proceed
    • If not found return 404 with message stating all recipient is not found.
  • If all fields are filled out, then find the recipient. 
    • If it is found, check the fields are all the same. 
      • If they match - proceed
      • If they don't match return a 400 error with message that prompts the caller to use the Update Recipient endpoint because the details are different
    • If it is not found, create the recipient

This should:
  1. Create (or find) the recipient. 
    Note: find by "reference" field within the consumer organisation's recipients
  2. Create the data request
  3. Send to the recipients

Title: Work-Record does not have a crop
Description:
According to the JSON Schema, a /crop/work-record resource should have a Crop attached to the Product Components:
https://github.com/Pure-Farming/moa-platform-documentation/blob/main/standardisation_schemas/dereferenced/crop/work-record.json#L2079-L2138

This doesn't currently exist in the database... so:
  1. Create a PDS migration that adds the crops field (this is likely to be a jsonb field on the crop_work_record_logged_operations_products_components table)
  2. Modify the resource_type.work_record view (drop and re-create) to include the new column
  3. Update the change-detection to include the crops field
  4. Update the data-writer to write the incoming crops field
  5. Update the dapper query in the Output Data API to include the crops field (and create the necessary mapper)

Title: Resource types: Work Record
Resource types: Livestock & movements
/livestock/movement/animal-birth-registration Real Change Detection
Description:
This involves inspecting the primary datastore and determining whether the incoming data represents a change of state of the overall system or not. It must check field by field for equivalence (that the new data being written would not cause a change) - this is not quite the same as checking that it exactly matches.
In the change detection lambda function:
  • inspect all fields
    • See if there's a change / new record
    • If it doesn't exist - send immediately to data-writer SQS 
    • If it does exist - check all fields
      • Upon first field difference send to data-writer SQS
      • If all fields match - don't send to data-writer
Acceptance Criteria:
If incoming data is already in database (same event) it should not get sent to the data-writer
If incoming data is updated it should get sent to the data-writer
If incoming data is new is should get sent to the data-writer

  • When the data is uploaded it creates a data-connection
    • This marks the Farm Focus connector as live
  • HOWEVER, because this is a manual process, next year when the farmer goes to connect up Farm Focus to provide the next years data
    • The connector is already marked as live and cannot be edited
    • The farmer will then be unable to load in the next years data
What needs to happen:
  • Either...
    • Expire the farm focus connector after 8-10 months so the farmer can re-connect the new years data, OR
    • Have the ability for the Farmers to add more files and process those to the existing
Acceptance Criteria: Farmers can upload a new files for a new year (after previously connecting Farm Focus)

Title: [Easy Dairy] Deletions
Description:
As a farmer and data consumer I want the data from Easy Dairy to be accurate so that I can rely on it
  • If a farmer deletes a record it should be deleted in Pure Farming
Approach:
  • When a Provider user with the same holding and org re-uploads their DIF file compare the uploaded data against the last upload of data (compare what we have in the standardised bucket from the previous upload)
  • If a record is missing from the new file AND it is in the timespan covered by the previous upload:
    • Assume the record is deleted - send deleted event (deletion lambda) to add a deleted flag to the data
    • Done via fire event to the change detection lambda 
This will apply to:

  • icarAnimalCoreResource
  • icarMovementArrivalEventResource
  • icarMovementBirthEventResource
  • icarMovementDepartureEventResource
  • icarMovementDeathEventResource
  • pfStockReconciliation
Acceptance Criteria:
Deleting a record in Easy Dairy will mean that the record is deleted in Pure Farming's PDS (soft delete)
Modifying a record in Easy Dairy will mean that the original record in Pure Farming is deleted and a new record is created (within the same datapoint key)

Title: [SAPUTO] Write ingestion - Fetch CSV export data
Description:
As a Farmer I want to be able to provide my milk pickup CSV export data to a data consumer so that they can receive pfMilkCollection data.

Initially Saputo will provide an exported CSV from their MilkRoom application.  This will be a one-off export of all farms and all pickups. 

We will drop the file into an S3 bucket (Drop bucket).  This story addresses this case.


Saputo plan to have an API developed soon and when it is available PF will ingest the milk collection data from the Saputo API.
Scope:

  • When a farmer connects via provider, it sends a event to the lambda with the data connection id. 
  • Update the fetch lambda : If there isn't an S3 event and object key THEN lookup the data connection id and find the latest file in moa.ingestion.saputo.

Drop path (Drop):          moa.ingestion.saputo/<drop-date>/<saputo_csv_export>.csv
Destination path (RAW):   saputo/<processing_date>/milk-collection_<universal_id>.csv
Acceptance Criteria: The files written to the raw bucket and the fetch event is published

Title: Set CODEOWNERS on PR / Set global ruleset for all repos
[Stock Reconciliation] 2 Write ingestion - standardisation - pfStockReconciliation
Description:
As a data requester,
I want to access a summarised view of livestock movements for a specific farm,
So that I can evaluate the farm's operational performance and make informed decisions.
We need to make enhancements and modify the existing ap-southeast-2-stock-rec-projection-transformation lambda. 
Mapping for the standardisation is available here:

Lambda triggers and where it writes the data will need to be preserved. We are only changing the standardisation process. 
Once the new process has been added, remove existing. 



 

2.      Obtain the time zone of the entity/holding. Use timezone to determine hemisphere. If NZ and Aus timezones Hemisphere = Sothern, if ELSE northern. 

 

3.      Get all animals related entity id

    1. Values: species, gender, animal id, birth date, is deleted = FALSE, status

 

4.      For Northern hem:

Birthing period = Any births between 1st of July 2025 of current year till 30th of June 2026 of next year is the birthing period of current year.

e.g. Birthing period for 2025

                a- 2 July 2025

                b- 27 Feb 2026

e.g. Birthing period for 2024:

                a- 28 June 2025

                b- 2 Dec 2024

5.      For southern hem:

Birthing period = the year the animal was born. Which it between 1st of Jan till 31st of Dec of current year.

If the hemisphere cannot be determined or is unknows, by default use Southern hemisphere.

 

6.      Group by

    1. Species + gender + birth period

 

7.      In the list of animals above, use the animal identifier and entity id to get all of the event details:

    1. Event, event date time (this needs to be converted to local date time), is deleted = null
    2. This needs to be grouped by inventory class and month.

 

Creating the stock recon

We create the stock recon by starting with the current month then work backwards up to the last month of which an event occurred. Stock recon is done for each inventory class.

For the first month (current month):

8.      To determine the current number of animals on a farm = sum of all animals in an inventory class where status = alive. This will be the closing number of the current month.

9.      Events = all the events of the inventory class for the month.

10. Opening = Closing + deaths + departures – Births – arrivals.

 

For the previous months:

11. Closing = following month’s opening

12. Opening = Closing + deaths + departures – Births – arrivals.

This needs to be looped until we get to the last month of which an event has occurred, OR upto 36 months (with current month = first month)

Title: [Rabobank NZ] Add user to entity service
Description:
A new Rabobank user has attempted to get to discovery without being set up in PF yet. 

So the user has been created in Keycloak via SSO, but not in PF. So they see a blank screen in that case. Miranda can't create the user via AMBOSS because it is already in Keycloak and the user service Add user endpoint attempts to create the user in keycloak as well and fails if it is already there. 

So we need to create the user via the entity service add user endpoint (this requires to lookup the keycloakId in keycloak for the  user) and Miranda can then connect the user to Rabobank Org via AMBOSS.

The user is katie.rodwell@rabobank.com

Title: [Arla] Discuss with Rob and Rich M. how to enable Arla to know when a farmer has removed consent for data
Description:
We could have an additional lambda listen to the PII files being dropped. This lambda can compare Arla's existing recipients/holdings/connections (pick one) with the content of the pii file and mark the recipients that belong to missing units as hidden. This would allow Arla getting existing and non existing recipients in one API call and they could even tell when the access was taken away.

This is enough if Arla still needs access to the data of the farms that they already have. If they can't have access to existing farm data for Farms that have declined access now we will need to discsuss further because that would be different to Sainsbury's current behaviour.

There is a catch up planned. :)

Title: [Trev] standardisation times out occasionally
[SAPUTO] Write ingestion - Pre-process
Description:
A pre-process step is required.

Each farm needs to be put into a separate JSON file.
A farm will typically have multiple pickups (Multiple rows per farmId)

We will only separate the JSON per-farm AFTER the farmer has connected to the data source

Incoming path (RAW path):   saputo/<processing_date>/milk-collection_<universal_id>.csv
Destination path (Preprocessing bucket):  saputo/<processing_date>/dairy/milk-collection/f_<farm_number>_e_<entity_id>_u_<universal_id>.json

e.g.:
 {"farmid":1234, "rows";["row 1"]},

Note:
Verify that we can process 500 rows in one go. Otherwise we will need to add pagination
Acceptance Criteria: Dropped file is separated in different JSONs in Pre-pros bucket

Title: [SAPUTO] Write ingestion - Standardise
Description:
As a Farmer I want to provide my Saputo milk collection (i.e. pickup data) to a data consumer so that they can receive PF pfMilkCollection data
  1. Pick up the file from the pre-processed or raw bucket
Create a milk collection observation for each pickup event in the Saputo data
Mapping:

The mapping information can be found in the SharePoint mapping folder Saputo.  The files include:
  • MapSaputoMilkCollection.json - the mapping file
  • Mapping Notes.docx - explanation of some important aspects of the mapping
  • SampleSaputoMilkCollection.csv - an artificial sample data file create by Krishna Kattula at Saputo
  • SampleSaputoMilkCollectionPF.json - example of a PF JSON record for a milk collection event.
The mapping notes define a method for determining Pure Farming's "productionDays". 

The process for determining production days requires the previous Saputo pickup record for the farm.
The first pickup from the farm for the season is an exception (see notes).
Note that Saputo plan to have an API developed soon.

Destination path:  dairy/milk-collection/<drop_date>/saputo/e_<entity_id>_u_<universal_id>.json

Note: The names of the analysis results should aligned with Synlait/ Fonterra
Acceptance Criteria:
• Standardised file is created/mapped correctly according to the mapping file MapSaputoMilkCollection.json
  • Standardised logs are without errors

[Sainsburys and Arla] Automate Create an ECS container
Description:
As a dev 
I want to automate the refresh task of the materialised views that are specific to Sainsburys and Arla data
So that the API queries are not dependent on the devs.

Create an ECS container. This is just a Hello world container


Overall solution:





Background:
To fix this issue, we have created a series of materialised views that are specific to Sainsburys and Arla data. For these two customers, the data API queries these views instead of querying the normal resource type view. These materialised views need to be refreshed every day, so that Sainsburys and Arla see the latest data. Currently, on the Ireland bastion host in prod, there are two scripts (sainsburys_refresh.sql & arla_refresh.sql) that do this. Another advantage of using these views if that we can control what data is presented to the customer; for example, if a problem is found in the data in the DB, we could delay refreshing that resource type view under the data is fixed.

The materialised views are (under the resource_type schema):

Sainsburys:

livestock_animal_sainsburys
enterprise_statistics_sainsburys
livestock_movement_animal_arrival_sainsburys
livestock_movement_animal_birth_registration_sainsburys
livestock_movement_animal_death_sainsburys
livestock_movement_animal_departure_sainsburys

Arla:

livestock_animal_arla
enterprise_statistics_arla
livestock_movement_animal_arrival_arla
livestock_movement_animal_birth_registration_arla
livestock_movement_animal_death_arla
livestock_movement_animal_departure_arla

We need a way to automatically refresh these views. The views also have to refreshed concurrently, so that reads / writes are not blocked on the underlying table. The views support that, but they are very slow because of the amount of data being refreshed. For this reason, we can't use a lambda function to do the refresh, as we do with the permissioned view. 

So, we need to:

1) work out how to refresh these views, eg ECS task etc
2) work when the views should be refreshed, ie daily at x time, staged etc
3) do we want a table to control the refresh; ie, if we have a table that listed the views, there could be a enable/disable flag that can turn off the refresh.
Acceptance Criteria: Container exists

Title: [Appraise] Users are asked to sign in again when being redirected to Provider
[Medium] WA-02 No Server-Side Anti-Malware Protection
Description:
As PF Ingestion,
I want every file uploaded for ingestion to be scanned for malware
So that the system remains secure, data integrity is maintained, and we prevent malicious files from compromising the ingestion process or downstream systems.

Miroboard for discussion: https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVJvXvxTI=/?share_link_id=604866352391

**Description**
Applications should generally scan uploaded files with anti-malware software to ensure that they do not contain
anything malicious.

**Systems Affected**
The following systems were affected:
• https://ux.api.test.purefarming.com/provider/statement/26eabd4f-494e-4f45-9528-1d1dc62b863e

**Impact and Severity**
The application allows uploading of an EICAR test file which is a benign malware detected by anti-viruses.

**Recommendation**
To remediate this finding:
• Ensure that files uploaded are continuously monitored by an up-to-date malware scanner. Any file uploaded should be scanned for malware before it becomes available to the users. Files failing the scan should be instantly quarantined, preventing harm to the end users.

**Reference**
For more information on this finding:
• OWASP - https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/10-Business_Logic_Testing/09-Test_Upload_of_Malicious_Files

**CVSS**
For the CVSS score calculation:
• https://www.first.org/cvss/calculator/3.1#CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N

**Reproduction**
To reproduce the issue, using a proxy such as Burp Suite:
• Log in as the 'miranda.hennebry+drtesting@rezare.com' user, then click on 'D Ramos Holding.'
• Navigate to 'Enterprise' and select 'Connect Data and Connect.' In the options menu, choose 'Upload PDF Statement.' Upload the EICAR document from this section.Figure 2 eicar pdf was uploaded.
• Verify that EICAR pdf was uploaded.

Figure 3 virus total has identified pdf as malicious

Title: [Overseer][Appraise] Update MetaDataSourceInformation to support the Appraise consumer org flow
Description:
As a third-party software (like Appraise), I want to be able to provide data sources with additional information about where the original data request comes from (for example, ASB).

This is currently not possible for Appraise because MetaDataSourceInformation is set on ASB and not Appraise, also Appraise needs to be able to "forward" the setting of any underlying consumer organisation.

To achieve this we will need to enable the addition of the relevant information to the data request recipient, so it can be set by Appraise and be read out by Provider.

Scope:
  • Update data request recipient to have a field MetaDataSourceInformation as we have already on Consumer Orgs (see #35685)
    • Add this field to CRUD endpoints
  • Update Ecosystem /ecosystem/application/data-request/{dataRequestId} endpoint to allow MetaDataSourceInformation  to be set
  • Update Provider to check on the data request recipient for MetaDataSourceInformation if it is not already on the Consumer Organisation
    • In case there is MetaDataSourceInformation set on Organisation AND data request recipient use the MetaDataSourceInformation  on the data request recipient ( @Andrew Cooke do you agree?)
  • Update Developer Hub Ecossystem Area to reflect those changes, so Appraise (or others) can use it as intended

Background:
As per #35685 the consumer organisations have been updated to include metadata for different data sources.  The Overseer data connection UI is currently using the metadata on the consumer organisation to check if the provider is able to connect Overseer for this data request, as well as what the publishing destination used in Overseer should be for this request.

However the implemented flow breaks when the request is being forwarded via the Appraise application as it resends the request via its own consumer organisation.  In the Test environment we have currently gotten around this by adding the data source metadata for the ASB publish destination onto the Appraise consumer org, this will not be viable long term however.

Update the behaviour of this data source metadata such that the Appraise application is able to set it correctly when it is forwarding data requests on behalf of multiple different consumer organisations.
Acceptance Criteria:
The current behavior of Overseer data connection UI is maintained so that we are able to give the right publishing destination and save the right client information against the data connection for accessing the Overseer API.
Appraise is able to send data requests for multiple different organsations with different Overseer publishing destinations and API client credentials such that Purefarming is successfully able to create the data connections and retrieve the data from Overseer.

Title: [Muddyboots] Turn off retries/prod-alert-notifications for 403s
Description:
##Scope:
1. If we get a 403 from muddyboots, we can treat it as an error that is not worth notifying/retrying

##Background:
Muddyboots connections process:
1. Farmer's side:
   - Farmer chooses Muddyboots as a data source in the provider portal
   - Farmer gets asked to log in to Muddyboots
   - Farmer shares data for his site with MapOfAg Organisation in MuddyBoots
   - Farmer copies the link of the page he is on when sharing the data and pastes it into the provider portal's current page
   - Farmer clicks connect and is done from his side

2. MoA side:
   - MapOfAg Organisation Admin needs to approve the share request
     - Why, I don't know, it seems weird, but it is the process in Muddyboots
     - The task is done by one of the people in the UK, likely Sarah or Coralie 

3. PF side:
   - As soon as a connection is established via the provider portal, PF attempts to fetch data for that farmer
   - Muddyboots is set up with an ingestion schedule of 'daily', which means that PF (re)fetches muddyboots data for that farmer every day

###Problem:
As long as the MoA admin has not yet approved the data share request in Muddyboots PF's attempt to fetch data will fail with 403 (unauthorised). Which happens (almost) each time after the farmer hits connect because it is very unlikely for the MoA admin to approve the share request exactly when the farmer has shared his data.

This causes a flood of error messages in our prod alerts channel as well as unnecessary retries of muddyboots ingestion.

Title: Add validation to Pii Onboarder to check naming convention using holding and org id
Description:
As **Sainsbury's**, I want to ensure that there is **only one PF holding per FarmMetrics Unit ID**, so that data integrity is maintained across the system.

When new connections are added to a holding:
*   Verify that the existing **Holding Identifier** and **Organisation Identifier** follow the naming convention defined by the **PII Holding Creator**.
*   **If the naming convention matches**:
    *   Append the relevant information to the onboarding file.
 *   **If the naming convention does not match** (This ensures valid holdings are processed successfully, while invalid holdings are flagged for manual review):
    *   Collect all errors during processing.
    *   Raise these errors at the end of the process.

##Background
*   The **PII Holding Creator** process is relatively new and introduced rules that were not present during Sainsbury's initial onboarding.
*   Initially, the naming convention for Sainsbury's Holdings and Organisations was different.
*   Historical holdings have been grouped under a single Organisation. However, we suspect that some units classified as “historical” may have returned, which could lead to duplicate holdings.

More detail:
**The Goal**

To enable the **PII Onboarder** in Production, which standardizes naming conventions to ensure every Farm and Unit (Holding) is uniquely identifiable and updatable by the Onboarding Service.

**The New Logic**

*   **Farm Identifier:** `Sainsburys_anonymous - Farm {farm_id}`
*   **Holding Identifier:** `anonymous - Unit {unit_id}`
*   **The Rule:** A Farm can contain multiple Units, but a Unit belongs to only one Farm. 

**The Problem: Legacy Data Mismatch**

The PII Onboarder cannot be turned on yet because the existing data in Production follows old, inconsistent rules. If enabled now, the system will fail to recognize existing records and create **duplicates** instead of updates. 

**Key Conflict Areas:**

1.  **"Catch-all" Organizations:** Previously, many distinct beef units were grouped under a single "Generic" Farm Organization. The new logic expects each Farm ID to have its own unique Organization.
2.  **Historic Units:** Older units that left the program were also bundled into a single Organization. If one returns, the new service will create a second, duplicate Farm/Holding entry rather than re-activating the old one.
3.  **Data Corruption:** Some existing Farm Organizations are currently linked to the wrong `farm_id` or are tied to multiple farms/units simultaneously, which violates the new 1-to-1 naming logic. 

**The Required Solution**

Before activation, a **data migration or cleanup** is necessary to: 

*   Rename existing Organizations/Holdings to match the `Sainsburys_anonymous...` convention.
*   "Unbundle" units that were grouped under generic organizations so they align with their specific `farm_id`.
*   Fix existing mapping errors where IDs are linked to the wrong entities.

Title: [Genus] 3.1 Add Internal Parameters to Data Source
Description:
1. Add new field "ConfigurationParameters" -> Can be JSON doc or KeyValue pairs
  2. Return ConfigurationParameters with GETs
  3. Update ConfigurationParameters with PUTs/PATCHes
  4. Remove ConfigurationParameters when DELETEing a data source
  5. Add ConfigurationParameters to POST/create
Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Creating a new data source with ConfigurationParameters saves the parameters
- [ ] Creating a new data source without ConfigurationParameters saves ConfigurationParameters as null
- [ ] Updating a data source with ConfigurationParameters = null removes all ConfigurationParameters from the data-source
- [ ] Updating a data source without specifying ConfigurationParameters at all does not change the ConfigurationParameters
- [ ] Updating a data source with ConfigurationParameters set, sets the ConfigurationParameters to the exact value specified (remove missing ones, update existing ones, add new ones)
- [ ] Data source ConfigurationParameters are not passed out through the Provider BFF
* /provider/data-connections
* /provider/data-sources/{dataSourceId}
* /provider/data-sources/findByDataRequestRecipientId
- [ ] The following endpoints return ConfigurationParameters if internal parameters are set, and do not return ConfigurationParameters if they are null
* GET /data-source/source/v2/{id}
* GET /data-source/source/v2/find-by-identifier
* PUT /data-source/source
* PATCH /data-source/source
* GET /data-source/source/v2

##Background:
**The Goal**

To enable the **PII Onboarder** in Production, which standardizes naming conventions to ensure every Farm and Unit (Holding) is uniquely identifiable and updatable by the Onboarding Service.

**The New Logic**

*   **Farm Identifier:** `Sainsburys_anonymous - Farm {farm_id}`
*   **Holding Identifier:** `anonymous - Unit {unit_id}`
*   **The Rule:** A Farm can contain multiple Units, but a Unit belongs to only one Farm. 

**The Problem: Legacy Data Mismatch**

The PII Onboarder cannot be turned on yet because the existing data in Production follows old, inconsistent rules. If enabled now, the system will fail to recognize existing records and create **duplicates** instead of updates. 

**Key Conflict Areas:**

1.  **"Catch-all" Organizations:** Previously, many distinct beef units were grouped under a single "Generic" Farm Organization. The new logic expects each Farm ID to have its own unique Organization.
2.  **Historic Units:** Older units that left the program were also bundled into a single Organization. If one returns, the new service will create a second, duplicate Farm/Holding entry rather than re-activating the old one.
3.  **Data Corruption:** Some existing Farm Organizations are currently linked to the wrong `farm_id` or are tied to multiple farms/units simultaneously, which violates the new 1-to-1 naming logic. 

**The Required Solution**

Before activation, a **data migration or cleanup** is necessary to: 

*   Rename existing Organizations/Holdings to match the `Sainsburys_anonymous...` convention.
*   "Unbundle" units that were grouped under generic organizations so they align with their specific `farm_id`.
*   Fix existing mapping errors where IDs are linked to the wrong entities.

Title: [Sainsbury's] not getting sire name in Animal Core parentage
Description:
Rob has mentioned that the Sire name is not available in sire details even if it was passed through via FM.

MArkus has done analysis:

Hi Rob,

 

I fear I need to put the breaks on a little bit on here. I have done some digging and have some clarification questions before we go ahead with a proposal for Sainsbury’s.

 

Observations:

  • FM Export’s ‘Sire Id’ can contain ear tags as well as names. I have only observed this with SDDG exports, I did not look into others yet.
  • It does not appear that we have any case (neither IB nor SDDG) where ‘Sire Id’ AND ‘Sire Name’ are populated
    • I did mention that in case of a ‘Sire Name’ AND ‘Sire Id’ being available the ‘Sire Id’ would be put in parentage.identiier.id and the sire’s name in parentage.officialName. I couldn’t find any animal with parentage.officialName being populated (this is now assuming PF is processing this correctly)
  • As Rich mentioned below

This check however creates a confusing output.

First, the ear tag check:

    • identifies ‘Sire Id’ values that start with UK, followed by a 12-digit number. Ear tags like this will be saved with parentage.identifier.scheme = ‘uk.cts.eartag’
    • Everything that is different to this will have parentage.identifier.scheme = ‘net.farmmetrics.sireId’

I have attached a list of parentage.identifer.id’s that we have in our db for parentage.identifier.scheme = ‘net.farmmetrics.sireId’, in other words values in ‘Sire Id’ that are not UK followed by a 12-digit number. We can see, amongst others, the following examples:

    • UK9331322563 (starts with UK, but followed by only a 10-digit number)
    • XHO6113 (id like names)
    • US74567945 (US eartags?)
    • 000540816136 (random numbers)
    • And other non-name like strings
    • But also strings that are obviously names, like ALADDIN

 

Are those observations Re: FM exports in alignment with your expectation?

 

Re: PF’s parentage.identifier.id values with parentage.identifier.scheme = ‘net.farmmetrics.sireId’:

  • Is the ear tag check complete? Do we only consider UK followed by 12-digit number as true ear tags?
  • Is there a rule that we can apply to distinguish true names from other forms of identifiers (other than UK ear tag)? Or let me rephrase that, would Sainsbury’s be satisfied by seeing US74567945/000540816136 as sire name?
  • Do we know if Sainsbury’s was told (back in the day) to distinguish between parentage.identifier.id based on parentage.identifier.scheme already?

 

I think that’s all for now. Sorry, this is so messy, I hope we can tidy that up a bit.

 

Cheers,

Markus

Title: [Sainsbury's] Group births by Gamehcanger/Kinemorny/or not
Description:
Sainsbury's was not quite sure if Kill Data is up to date in their database, so they asked us to provide the numbers of kills per month. Rob wanted this enriched to have the number of gamechanger animals, kinemorny animals and animals that are not gamechanger/kinemorny per month, so something like this:
| Month | Scheme | Number of Carcass Observation Animals |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| 2026-01 | Overall | 1234 |
| 2026-01 | Gamechanger | 1200 |
| 2026-01 | Kinemorny | 30 |
| 2026-01 | x | 4 |

It turns out Saisnbury's does not have the same numbers, but they think it's because of the animal-assessment endpoint (which holds information about gamechanger/kinemorny) rather than the kill data itself. To verify they want the same thing as above for Birth Registrations.

SQL Query that has been used for kill data:

```sql
WITH animal_classification AS (
    SELECT
        laae_animal_identifier_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN BOOL_OR(laae_assessment = 'gamechanger') THEN 'gamechanger'
            WHEN BOOL_OR(laae_assessment = 'kinermony') THEN 'kinermony'
            ELSE ''
        END AS derived_assessment
    FROM
        resource_type.livestock_animal_assessment_event
    GROUP BY
        laae_animal_identifier_id
)
SELECT
    TO_CHAR(co.lcoe_event_date_time, 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
    COALESCE(ac.derived_assessment, '') AS assessment,
    COUNT(DISTINCT co.lcoe_animal_identifier_id) AS "number of animal"
FROM
    resource_type.livestock_carcass_observation_event co
LEFT JOIN
    animal_classification ac ON co.lcoe_animal_identifier_id = ac.laae_animal_identifier_id

GROUP BY
    1, 2
ORDER BY
    1 DESC, 2 ASC;
```
Acceptance Criteria:
Create a csv export that looks like this 

| Month | Scheme | Number of Birth Registrations |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| 2026-01 | Overall | 1234 |
| 2026-01 | Gamechanger | 1200 |
| 2026-01 | Kinemorny | 30 |
| 2026-01 | x | 4 |

for Sainsbury's Birth Registrations until 2025-01 (including)

see sql query for kill data in description

Title: Add in holdings to AMBOSS
Description:
Adds the ability to view holdings and recipients for organisations

 When viewing a Provider Organisation you can see and search through holdings within the organisation  When viewing a Consumer Organisation you can see and search through the recipients within the organisation

View a holdings details

Clicking on the holding name will pull up the details for the holding

  1. List of holding details with the ability to edit the details
  2. List of which users have access to this holding
  3. List of connected data sources and when they were connected
  4. List of recipients attached to this holding (a list of consumer organisations that reference this holding)

View a recipients details

Clicking on the recipients name will pull up the details for the recipient

  1. The details of the consumer's recipient
  2. The data requests that have been sent to this recipient
  3. The consumer organisation that created this recipient
  4. The holding that the recipient points to

We are contacting you as we have identified that your AWS Account currently has one or more AWS Lambda functions using the Node.js 20.x runtime.

We are ending support for Node.js 20.x in Lambda on April 30, 2026. This follows Node.js 20.x End-Of-Life (EOL) scheduled on April 30, 2026 [1]. End of support does not impact function execution. Your functions will continue to run. However, they will be running on an unsupported runtime which is no longer maintained or patched by the AWS Lambda team.

As described in the Lambda runtime support policy [2], end of support for language runtimes in Lambda happens in several stages.

- Starting on April 30, 2026, Lambda will no longer apply security patches and other updates to the Node.js 20.x runtime used by Lambda functions, and functions using Node.js 20.x will no longer be eligible for technical support. Also, Node.js 20.x will no longer be available in the AWS Console, although you can still create and update functions using Node.js 20.x via AWS CloudFormation, the AWS CLI, AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM), or other tools.

- Starting August 31, 2026, you will no longer be able to create new Lambda functions using the Node.js 20.x runtime.
- Starting September 30, 2026, you will no longer be able to update existing functions using the Node.js 20.x runtime.

We recommend that you upgrade your existing Node.js 20.x functions to the latest available Node.js runtime in Lambda before April 30, 2026.

Your impacted Lambda functions using the Node.js 20.x runtime are listed on the 'Affected resources' tab.

To find all such functions in your account, repeat the following command for each region:

aws lambda list-functions --region us-east-1 --output text --query "Functions[?Runtime=='nodejs20.x'].FunctionArn"

Starting 180 days before deprecation, you can also use Trusted Advisor to identify functions using the Node.js 20.x runtime [4].

If you have any concerns or require further assistance, please contact AWS Support [5].

Title: Trivy Supply-Chain Compromise
Description:
Anyone who is using Trivy for scanning should immediately disable it until we can ascertain that it is safe. 

Earlier in the month (10ish days ago) Trivy was compromised in a sophisticated supply chain attack - primarily as far as I can tell against the docker images, but may also affect binary versions. 

See the link below for more information - I will post follow up in the thread.
https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/03/24/detecting-investigating-defending-against-…

Source: 
Daniel Buchanan: Trivy Compromised - Disable Immediately | Rezare Systems > General | Microsoft Teams
Acceptance Criteria:
Disable Trivy in all pipelines - merge into main
We do not need to dig into which pipeline runs may be affected - Daniel B is working on that
